Pak Study MCQs · pages 151-200

Sharpen your Pak Study recall

Tap a card to reveal the explanation. Take it one seal at a time.

500 questions in this set
1

Who presented the six points formula?

Correct Answer: Shaikh Mujeeb ur Rehman
Show explanation
Explanation

In 1966 Mujib-ur-Rehman (leader of the Awami League) made public his controversial six-point formula.

Sheikh Mujibur Rahman announced the six points in Lahore.

2

Which was the official language declared in 1956 constitution?

Correct Answer: Both A & B
Show explanation
Explanation

Constitution of 1956 · Pakistan was declared as the Islamic Republic

while

Both Urdu and Bengali were declared as official and national languages of Pakistan in the 1956 constitution

              All important questions of  "the 1956 constitution"

              1956 constitution is promulgated on 23 March 1956

              ***

              Iskander Mirza abrogated the 1956 constitution

              ***

              In 1956 the designation of Governor General changed to President

              ****

              40 years was the age prescribed for president in the 1956 constitution

              **

              The Constitution of 1956 contained 234 articles divided into thirteen parts and six schedules

              **

              The constitution of 1956 remained implemented for 2 years 6 months and 14 days.

              **

              The constitution of 1956 provided a Parliamentary form of government for the country.

              **

              1956 constitution legislature was Unicameral

              **

              The Constitution of 1956 provided for a federal system with the principle of parity between East Pakistan and West Pakistan

              ND16-1-2023

              ****

              nd9-2-2023

              ND 12-2-2023

              SH26-8-2023

              3

              World War-I was fought between:

              Correct Answer: 1914-1918
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              World War I was a global war originating in Europe 

              This war lasted from 28 July 1914 to 11 November 1918.

              Chlorine was First used during World War I at Ypres in April 1915

              After World War 1 influenza disease occurred in 1918

              During the First World War, Russia signed the Peace Treaty (1917) with Germany.

              ***

              World War II or the Second World War, was a global war

              This war started in 1939 and ended in 1945



              4

              When Mohammad Ali Bogra presented Bogra Formula in the assembly?

              Correct Answer: October 1953
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Bogra Formula was a political compromise presented and proposed by Prime Minister Bogra on 7 October 1953

              The Bogra Formula sought to permanently abolish the Governor-General and to be replaced with the elected figurehead presidency.

              Mohammad Ali Bogra was the 2nd Bengali to serve as Prime Minister of Pakistan.

              Sahibzada Mohammad Ali Bogra was the 3rd Prime Minister of Pakistan from 17 April 1953 till 12 August 1955.

              He was the 3rd & 7th Minister of Foreign Affairs from 13 June 1962 till 23 January 1963

              Bogra was Minister of Defence from 17 April 1953 to 24 October 1954.

              ND5-07-2023


              5

              Which Pakistani Scientist was awarded Nobel Prize for physics in 1979?

              Correct Answer: Dr Abdus Salam
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              In 1979,  Dr. Abdul Salam won the Nobel Prize for physics.

              He was the second Muslim and first Pakistani scientist to get the Nobel Prize.


              He won 12 Awards with his wife.

              1. Copley Medal: 1990
              2. Royal Medal: 1978
              3. Hughes Medal: 1964
              4. Pride of Performance: 1958
              5. Nishan-e-Imtiaz
              6. Adams Prize: 1958
              7. Lomonosov Gold Medal: 1983
              8. Atoms for Peace Award
              9. Gold Medal for Outstanding Contributions to Physics: 1981
              10. Smith's Prize for Essay: 1950
              11. Jožef Stefan Medal: 1980
              12. Cristoforo Colombo Prize: 1992

              Additional information 

              As of 2018, there have been two Pakistanis who have won the Nobel Prize.

              • First was Pakistani physicist Abdus Salam
              • Second was Malala Yousafzai
              6

              The most beautiful stone Marble is extracted from which province of Pakistan?

              Correct Answer: Khyber Pakhtunkhwa
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Stone-Marble is founded in Chilas District of KPK in huge quantities and Federally Administrated Tribal Areas (FATA)

              KPK produces around 60percent of total Pakistan's marble 

              Turkmenistan country`s capital is known as the 'White-Marble' City 


              7

              Which of these is not of the five rivers of Punjab?

              Correct Answer: Kunhar
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Kunhar River is  located in the Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province, northern Pakistan.

              Its previous name was NainSukh.

              Length : 166 kilometres 

              8

              Karachi faced which climate issue?

              Correct Answer: Heatwaves
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Karachi often experiences extreme heatwaves, especially during summer months.

              These heatwaves can cause health hazards, power shortages, and water scarcity.

              9

              The oldest mountains of the world are present in India and youngest mountain of the world are present in?

              Correct Answer: Pakistan
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Aravalli Range, an eroded stub of ancient mountains, is the oldest range of fold mountains in India.

               

              The Himalayan Mountain is the youngest range of Pakistan

              It's thought that this range formed about 40 million years ago

               It is located between Tibetan plateau and Ganga plain
              10

              In which province of Pakistan Mirani dam is being constructed?

              Correct Answer: Balochistan
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Mirani Dam is in Kech District in the Balochistan province of Pakistan.

              ﻿Mirani Dam is located in the Dashat river



              Mirani Dam was completed in July 2006 and began impounding the Dasht River in August 2006.


              The feasibility report of the Mirani dam project was first completed in 1956. 


              Mirani Dam was commissioned by(WAPDA) in 2001 to provide water for Gwadar city. 

              The Length of Mirani Dam is 3,350 ft (1,020 m)

              *************

              11

              How many seats were won by the Awami league in the 1970 election?

              Correct Answer: 160
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • 160 seats was won by awami league in the 1970 election
              • ***
                • In the elections of 1970, the Awami League won 167 of 169 East Pakistan seats in the National Assembly
                • but none of West Pakistan's 138 seats.
                • It also won 288 of the 300 provincial assembly seats in East Pakistan.

              12

              Who transformed the Muslim League into a Mass Movement?

              Correct Answer: Muhammad Ali Jinnah
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Muhammad Ali Jinnah turned the Muslim League from an elite political party into a mass-based movement.

              This transformation was crucial for the creation of Pakistan in 1947.

              13

              The capital of the Mughal Empire was shifted from Agra to Delhi by ________?

              Correct Answer: Shahjehan
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The capital of the Mughal empire was shifted from Agra to Delhi by Shah Jahan in the year of 1638.

              The new capital was officially named Shahjahanabad in 1648.

              It is now known as Old Delhi. 

              The architect behind the Taj Mahal, Ustad Ahmad Lahori, is credited with designing the Red Fort.

              14

              Which of the following mineral deposits are found in Potwar Plateau?

              Correct Answer: All of these
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Potwar Plateau includes parts of the districts of Jhelum, Chakwal and Rawalpindi.

              The Pothohar plateau is rich in minerals like gypsum, limestone, coal, oil and rock salt.

               

              *************

              15

              Which country is in the north west of Pakistan?

              Correct Answer: Afghanistan
              Show explanation
              Explanation


              16

              Which dam is not in Punjab? Mangla Dam, Chashma Dam, Rasul Dam, Tarbela Dam

              Correct Answer: Tarbela Dam
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Chashma Dam (Barrage) is located in the Mianwali District of the Punjab province.

              Mangla Dam is situated on the Jhelum River and lies partially in the Jhelum District of the Punjab province and partially in the Mirpur District of Azad Kashmir.

              Rasul Dam (Barrage) is on the River Jhelum, located between the Jhelum District and Mandi Bahauddin District, both in the Punjab province.

              Tarbela Dam is an earth-fill dam located on the Indus River in the Haripur District of the Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (KPK) province. 

              17

              Which river provides water to Sindh?

              Correct Answer: Indus River
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Indus River is also known as the Sindhu River or Darya-e-Sindh.

              It is the primary source of water for the Sindh province of Pakistan.

              Often described as the province's "lifeline".

              Because it sustains the region's agriculture, economy, and environment. 

              18

              Who was honored with Nishan-e-Haider for his bravery in 1965 Indo-Pak War?

              Correct Answer: Raja Aziz Bhatti
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Raja Aziz Bhatti was awarded the Nishan-e-Haider (Lit.: Emblem of Lion) for his actions of valor during the Battle of Burki in the Indo-Pak War of 1965.

              Major Raja Aziz Bhatti was the 1st PMA long course holder who also received the Sword of Honor and Norman medal

              while 

              Nishan-e-Haider's First award: Muhammad Sarwar

              Nishan-e-Haider's Last award: Havildar Lalak Jan

              19

              Name the palace that is the official residence of the President of Pakistan?

              Correct Answer: Aiwan-e-Sadr
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Aiwan-e-Sadr is the official residence and workplace of the President of Pakistan.

              It is located in Islamabad.

              It is s used for state ceremonies and official meetings.

              20

              One Unit was established in West Pakistan in ______?

              Correct Answer: September 30, 1955
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              On 30 September, 1955

              Until 1970

              Idea of Muhamad Ali Bogra ,named Bogra Formula.

              Implemented by Ch. Muhammad Ali


              agm.04.03.23


              21

              What was the first capital of the Suri Empire founded by Sher Shah Suri?

              Correct Answer: Sasaram
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Sher Shah Suri's initial or primary capital was Sasaram (in modern-day Bihar). 

              He also established the city of Shergarh (previously Humayun's Din Panah, now the Purana Qila area) as a capital during his reign in Delhi.

              22

              The Establishment of Delhi Sultanate begin with the invasion of _____?

              Correct Answer: Muhammad Ghouri
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              In 1192, the Afghan warrior Muhammad Ghori defeated the Rajput Confederacy, led by Prithviraj Chauhan, near Tarain.

              After Ghori's death in 1206, his generals divided the Ghurid territories and established the Delhi Sultanate.

              Qutb ud-Din Aibak, a slave soldier and Ghori's army commander, became the new sultan of Delhi. 

              23

              The highest official rank of Pakistan Army is ________?

              Correct Answer: General
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • Pakistan Army: The highest official rank is Field Marshal, though it is rarely awarded (e.g., General Ayub Khan in 1965). The highest active rank is General.
                • Pakistan Navy: The highest rank is Admiral.
                • Pakistan Air Force: The highest rank is Air Chief Marshal.
                • Police: The highest rank is Inspector General of Police (IGP), overseeing provincial or national law enforcement.
                24

                CNIC stands for _______?

                Correct Answer: Computerized National Identity Card
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                CNIC Stands for Computerized National Identity Card

                The CNIC was introduced in 2000

                NADRA Founded: March 10, 2000, Islamabad

                NADRA Headquarters: Islamabad
                ***

                History

                Pakistan initiated its National Identity Card System in 1973 under Article 30 of the Second Amendment.

                The first NIC being that of Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto, the Prime Minister of the time

                25

                Who is the highest wicket taker in ODI and Tests for Pakistan?

                Correct Answer: Wasim Akram
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Highest wicket-taker in ODI for Pakistan.

                Wasim Akram (502 wickets).

                Highest wicket-taker in T20 for Pakistan.

                Shadab Khan (104 wickets).

                Highest wicket-taker in TEST for Pakistan.

                Wasim Akram (414 wickets)

                26

                In which city Pakistan Ordinance Factory is located?

                Correct Answer: Wah
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The Pakistan Ordnance Factories (POF) is a major firearm and defense contractor

                headquartered in Wah Cantt, Punjab, Pakistan

                The POF was founded by the Government of Pakistan

                Founded 1951

                Owner: Pakistan Ministry of Defence



                27

                How many nautical miles does Pakistan include in its Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ)?

                Correct Answer: 200 nmi
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Pakistan’s EEZ extends 200 nautical miles from its coastline.

                Within this zone, Pakistan has exclusive rights over marine resources and economic activities.

                28

                The Single National Curriculum (SNC) in Pakistan was implemented at the primary level in ______?

                Correct Answer: 2021
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                SNC was first applied to primary classes (1–5) in 2021 across Pakistan.
                The second phase started in March 2022 for classes 6–8, and later for classes 9–12.
                29

                Pakistan lies between the longitudes _______?

                Correct Answer: 61 E to 75.5 E
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                Pakistan lies between latitudes 24°N to 37°N.
                Pakistan lies between longitudes 61°E to 75.5°E.
                Pakistan is divided into five physiographical divisions.
                Islamabad is the capital of Pakistan.
                The construction of Islamabad was completed in 1966.
                The total number of longitudes is 360.
                There are 179 eastern longitudes and 179 western longitudes.
                The Prime Meridian (0° longitude) is known as the GMT (Greenwich Mean Time) line.
                GMT line divides the Eastern and Western Hemispheres.
                30

                Who was the first and the last Chief Executive in the history of Pakistan?

                Correct Answer: General Parvaiz Musharraf
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                The first and last person to hold this title was General Pervez Musharraf. 
                First Chief Executive: General Pervez Musharraf took the title of Chief Executive after overthrowing Prime Minister Nawaz Sharif in a bloodless coup on October 12, 1999.
                Last Chief Executive: He held this position until he formally appointed himself President of Pakistan in June 2001.
                He later voluntarily transferred the powers of chief executive to Prime Minister Zafarullah Khan Jamali in 2002. This made him both the first and last person to hold the specific designation of Chief Executive. 
                31

                Name the Pakistani police inspector who won gold medal in Powerlifting Championship 2025 by defeating her Indian rival?

                Correct Answer: Irum Khanum
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Pakistani (Punjab) police female Inspector Irum Khanum won two gold medals at world powerlifting Championship 2025.

                She secured first position in both the powerlifting and deadlift events, defeating Indian competitor Sapna to claim the titles.

                She belongs to Rawalpindi Police. 


                Additional Information:

                The event was held in Colombo, Sri Lanka. The competition ran from November 27 to 30, 2025.

                ***

                32

                Who served as the President of Pakistan from 1971 to 1973?

                Correct Answer: Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto was a Pakistani barrister and politician who served as the ninth prime minister of Pakistan from 1973 to 1977, and prior to that as the fourth president of Pakistan from 1971 to 1973.

                34

                Who was the First Speaker of National Assembly of Pakistan?

                Correct Answer: Quaid-i-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The first Speaker of the National Assembly was Quid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah.

                Note

                Quaid-i-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah and Moulvi Tamizuddin Khan served as the President of the Constituent Assembly, a role similar to that of the Speaker, as the official title of 'Speaker' had not yet been established at that time.

                ***

                List of speakers of the National Assembly of Pakistan

                1. Quid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah (11-08-1947 to 11-09-1948)
                2. Maulvi Tamizuddin Khan (1948–1954)
                3. Abdul Wahab Khan (1955–1958)
                4. Muhammad Ibrahim Khan (1962–1965)
                5. Abdul Jabbar Khan (1965–1969)
                6. Sahibzada Farooq Ali (1973–1977)
                7. Malik Meraj Khalid (1977–1977)
                8. Fazal Elahi Chaudhry (1972–1973)
                9. Syed Fakhar Imam (1985–1986)
                10. Hamid Nasir Chattha (1986–1988)
                11. Malik Anwar Ali Noon (1988–1988)
                12. Gohar Ayub Khan (1990–1993)
                13. Yusuf Raza Gillani (1993–1997)
                14. Elahi Bux Soomro (1997–2001)
                15. Chaudhry Amir Hussain (2002–2008)
                16. Fahmida Mirza (2008–2013)
                17. Sardar Ayaz Sadiq (2013–2015, 2015–2018)
                18. Asad Qaiser (2018–2022)
                19. Raja Pervaiz Ashraf (2022–2024)
                20. Sardar Ayaz Sadiq (2024 to till date)

                Source Click here

                36

                Pakistan to celebrate ‘Surprise Day’ on _______?

                Correct Answer: 27 February
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                Pakistan Air Force celebrates Operation Swift Retort as Surprise on 27 February since 2020.

                After 14 February 2019, when suicide bomber attacked CRPF convoy in Pulwama, killing 40 CRPF soldiers, tensions between India and Pakistan escalated.

                Later on February 26, 2019 Indian Air Force (IAF) carried out counter-terror operation (Balakot Air Strike), hitting in Balakot.

                It was on February 27, 2019, when PAF exhibited supreme professionalism by shooting down two Indian Air Force jets, a MiG-21 Bison and a SU-30. The downed MiG-21 pilot, wing commander Abhinandan, whose aircraft had crashed inside Pakistan’s territory was captured.

                Abhinandan was later released and handed over to India on March 1, 2019.

                37

                When sixth Census was held in Pakistan?

                Correct Answer: 2017
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The sixth Census was held in Pakistan in 2017.

                Total Population of Pakistan in 2017 was 207.7 Million

                Total Males  106.34 Million

                Total Females 101.34 Million

                Growth rates 2.40%

                Literacy rate 59.13%.

                **************

                First Census was held in Pakistan in 1951.

                The second Census was held in Pakistan in 1961.

                The third Census was held in Pakistan in 1972.

                Fourth Census was held in Pakistan in 1981.

                The fifth Census was held in Pakistan in 1998.

                Sixth Census was held in Pakistan in 2017.

                Seventh in 2023


                38

                Rann of Kutch is a dispute area between Pakistani provence?

                Correct Answer: Sindh-India
                Show explanation
                Explanation
                • The Indo-Pakistani War of 1965 involved skirmishes between April and September 1965.
                  • Pakistan claims the 24th Parallel as the boundary in the Rann of Kutch, but India does not recognize it.
                  • The "Treaty of Rann of Kutch" was signed on 30 June 1965, resolving the territorial dispute.
                  39

                  Attock fort was built by _____?

                  Correct Answer: Akbar
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  • Attock Fort, built during the reign of Akbar from 1581 to 1583 at Attock Khurd.
                    • It was constructed to protect the River Indus's passage.
                    • It was built under the supervision of Khawaja Shamsuddin Khawafi.
                    • The fort features four gates: Delhi Gate, Lahori Gate, Kabuli Gate, and Mori Gate.
                    40

                    Annulment of partition of Bangal was made in _____?

                    Correct Answer: 1911
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Ø  On the visiting His Majesty King George V to Indo-Pakistan and holding of Darbar at Delhi on 12th December 1911 the partition of Bengal was cancelled.

                    Ø  ****

                    Ø  Partition of Bangal Announced on 19 July 1905 by Lord Curzon for Administrative requirements

                    Ø  Congress protest against Partition of Bangal
                    41

                    Under the Pakistan–KSA Prisoner Transfer Agreement, how many Pakistani prisoners will be transferred back to Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: 2107
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The agreement allows Pakistani prisoners to serve their remaining sentences in Pakistan.

                    A total of 2107 prisoners are covered under this transfer arrangement.

                    42

                    CPEC stands for _____?

                    Correct Answer: China Pakistan economic corridor
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    CPEC stands for China-Pakistan economic corridor

                    **

                    China–Pakistan Economic Corridor is a 3000 km framework of regional connection between China and Pakistan

                    Type of project------->Economic corridor

                    Location-------> Pakistan: Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Gilgit-Baltistan, Punjab, Balochistan, Sindh & Azad Kashmir, China: Xinjiang

                    Established---->20 April 2015 

                    ***


                    43

                    Which movement of East Pakistan also supported India in war of 1971?

                    Correct Answer: Mukti Bahini
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Mukti Bahini was the guerrilla resistance movement consisting of

                    • the Bangladeshi military,
                    • paramilitary and
                    • civilians

                    during the War of Liberation, which transformed East Pakistan into Bangladesh in 1971.


                    *******

                    ð  The war of 1971 between India and Pakistan lasted for 13 days from 3 December 1971 to the fall of Dacca (Dhaka) on 16 December 1971 .

                    ð  The war began with Operation Chengiz Khan's preemptive aerial strikes (india).

                    ð  on 16 December 1971 in Dhaka, marking the formation of East Pakistan as the new nation of Bangladesh.

                    ð  Officially, East Pakistan had earlier called for its secession from Pakistan on 26 March 1971.


                    44

                    In which agreement the ceasefire line in Kashmir was named Line of Control?

                    Correct Answer: Simla Agreement
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    According to the United Nations, the LOC came into effect in July 1972, after India and Pakistan fought a war that began in 1971.


                    They signed the Simla Agreement to define what was previously called the “ceasefire line.”

                    ***************

                    45

                    Who was the head of Awami League in 1970?

                    Correct Answer: Mujib ur Rehman
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Sheikh Mujib-Ur-Rehman served as the first President and later as the Prime Minister of Bangladesh from 17 April 1971 until his assassination on 15 August 1975.

                    46

                    Kharan desert is located in which province?

                    Correct Answer: Balochistan
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Kharan Desert is a sandy and mountainous desert situated in Balochistan Province in south-western Pakistan.

                    The Kharan desert in Balochistan near the border of Iran.

                    *************

                    47

                    Jahangir Khan is a famous player in which game?

                    Correct Answer: Squash
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Jahangir Khan is a former World No. 1 professional squash player from Pakistan.

                    He won the World Open title six times.

                    He won the British Open title ten times, from 1982 to 1991.

                    He was born into a Pashtun family from Neway Kelay Payan, Peshawar.

                    He served as President of the World Squash Federation from 2002 to 2008.

                    Jahangir Khan retired as a player in 1993.

                    48

                    Who is the writer of Urdu Book “Pakistan Naguzeer tha”?

                    Correct Answer: Syed Hasan Riaz
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Syed Hassan Raza is a Pakistani diplomat and a former senior career foreign service officer.

                    He served as the Special Secretary at Pakistan's Foreign Office, overseeing the Middle East, OIC & Legal divisions

                    49

                    What was the objective of Cripps Mission?

                    Correct Answer: To reform the constitutional issues
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Cripps Mission was a failed attempt in late March 1942 

                    The Cripps Mission was sent by the British government to India

                      to obtain Indian cooperation for the British war efforts in the 2nd World War.

                    The mission was headed by a senior minister Sir Stafford Cripps.

                    the objective of Cripps Mission: To reform the constitutional issues


                    **
                    ND13-1-2023


                    50

                    When was FATA merged into Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (KPK)?

                    Correct Answer: 2018
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    FATA was merged with KPK in May 2018.

                    FATA stands for Federally Administered Tribal Areas

                    FATA was merged with KPK under 25th amendment. 

                    After the merger of the Federally Administered Tribal Areas with KPK, FATA seats were abolished and there will be no election on 4 of the 8 seats. The rest of the 4 seats will also not be renewed in 2024.

                    51

                    Which of the following first President of the Constituent Assembly of Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: Quaid-e-Azam
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    On 11th August 1947 Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah was elected as the President of the Constituent Assembly of Pakistan.


                    The first session of the Constituent Assembly of Pakistan was held on 10th August 1947 at the Sindh Assembly Building in Karachi.


                    Liaquat Ali Khan headed after the death of Jinnah for three years and produced the Objectives Resolution.

                    ****


                    52

                    Who is the Head of the State of Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: President
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The President of Pakistan is the head of state of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan.


                    The current head of state of Pakistan is Asif Ali Zardari who was elected in 2024.


                    53

                    سب سے پہلے نعرہ “پاکستان کا مطلب کیا؟ لا الٰہ الا اللہ” کس نے لگایا؟

                    Correct Answer: اصغر صہبائی (اصغر سودائی)
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    یہ نعرہ سب سے پہلے اصغر صہبائی/اصغر سودائی نے دیا۔

                    بعد میں یہ نعرہ تحریکِ پاکستان کی پہچان بن گیا۔

                    54

                    Who completed the construction of Qutb Minar at Delhi?

                    Correct Answer: Iltutmish
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Qutub minar constructed by: Qutb Uddin Aibak

                    Construction of Qutub minar started in: 1199

                    Construction of Qutb Minar completed by: Iltutmish

                    55

                    One Unit Bill was passed in which year?

                    Correct Answer: 1955
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The One Unit was the reorganization of the provinces of Pakistan .

                    Prime Minister Muhammad Ali Bogra led it on 22 November 1954.

                    and passed on 30 September 1955

                    This program merged the four provinces of West Pakistan into a single province to parallel the province of East Pakistan

                    • West Punjab
                    • Sind
                    • NWFP
                    • Baluchistan

                    Additional information:

                    The One Unit scheme was dissolved on July 1, 1970

                    General Yahya Khan imposed Legal Framework Order No. 1970 to end the program

                    and reinstate the provisional status of the Four Provinces as of August 1947.

                    56

                    Which Mountain range is often called as the Third Pole?

                    Correct Answer: Hindu Kush Himalayan range
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The mountain range often called the "Third Pole" is the Hindu Kush Himalaya (HKH) region,

                    They contain the largest area of permanent ice cover outside of the Arctic and Antarctic. 

                     Additional information 

                    The Hindu Kush range starts near the Pamir Mountains,

                    The Tibetan Plateau (Pamir Plateau) is known as the Roof of the World.

                    Where the borders of Afghanistan, Pakistan, and China meet.

                    From this eastern point, it runs southwest through Pakistan and into Afghanistan. 

                    It is an 800-kilometre-long (500 mi) mountain range in Central and South Asia to the west of the Himalayas.

                    The highest peak of the Hindu Kush range in Pakistan is Tirich Mir

                    Elevation:  (7,708) meters (25,289) ft

                    57

                    When was the Constitution of 1973 enforced?

                    Correct Answer: 14th August 1973
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Constitution of 1973

                    Zulifqar Ali Bhutto introduced new constitution in Pakistan on August 14, 1973.

                    According to 1973 constitution, both national assembly and senate elect president for Pakistan whereas the prime minister is selected by national assembly.

                    Bicameral Legislature system was introduced for the first time in Pakistan in the constitution of 1973.

                    ******

                    1956 Constitution

                    March 23, 1956 by PM Chaudhry Mohammad Ali + GG Iskander Mirza

                    1962 Constitution

                    8th June 1962 by President Ayub Khan.

                    1973 Constitution

                    14th Aug 1973, by Zulfikar Ali Bhutto

                    58

                    Who was the first Chairman of Senate?

                    Correct Answer: Habib Ullah Khan
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    About Senate of Pakistan


                    Senate: Upper House of Pakistan

                    Senate in Urdu: ایوان بالا

                    Formation: 1973

                    Original members: 45

                    Raised to 63: 1977

                    Raised 87: 2 March, 1985

                    Raised to 100: 2002

                    First Hindu Woman Senator: Kirshna Kumari

                    Half of the Senate members retire after: 3 years

                    Members term: 6 years

                    Head of Senate is called: Chairman

                    National Assembly & Senate: Makes up the Parliament of Pakistan

                    Seats for each province: 23

                    Baluchistan Women Seats: 4

                    Sindh Women Seats: 4

                    KPK Women Seats: 4

                    Punjab Women Seats: 4

                    ICT Women Seat: 1

                    Total Women Seats: 17

                    Senate elections: after 3 years

                    First woman deputy chairperson: Dr Noor Jahan Panezai

                    Youngest chairman senate: Sadiq Sanjrani

                    First chairman Senate: Habib Ullah Khan




                    59

                    What is the northernmost administered point of Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: Tri-junction point
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The tri-junction point is where the borders of Pakistan, China, and India meet.

                    It is the northernmost point administered by Pakistan.

                    60

                    Which Range is called Roof of the World?

                    Correct Answer: Pamir Plateau
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Tibetan Plateau (Pamir Plateau) is known as the Roof of the World.

                    It's also the thickest crust in the world, between 70 and 90 kilometers.

                    The plateau is the world's largest and highest.

                    Its average elevation is more than 4,500 meters (14,764 feet).


                    Additional information 

                    The Shandur Pass in Pakistan is also sometimes called "the roof of the world".

                    61

                    Who was the political heir of Sir Syed Ahmad Khan?

                    Correct Answer: Nawab Mohsin-ul-Mulk
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Political and Institutional Heir of Sir Syed Ahmad Khan – Key Points

                    Heir: Nawab Mohsin-ul-Mulk

                    After Sir Syed’s death (1898): He became Secretary of the Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental (MAO) College.

                    Role: Led the Muhammadan Educational Conference and continued Sir Syed’s educational and political work.

                    Significance: Served as the immediate political and institutional successor of Sir Syed Ahmad Khan.

                    62

                    Which of the following political parties did not boycott the Simon Commission?

                    Correct Answer: All India Muslim League
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Simon's commission was rejected by the Indian National Congress and the Muslim League


                    The All India Muslim League divided into two groups.

                    • Jinnah Group
                    • Shafi Group

                    Quaid Group boycotted the Simon Commission but Shafi Group was against boycotting this commission.


                    Simon Commission was appointed in 1928


                    It comprises 7 members of the British


                    It was opposed by both Jinnah & Gandhi

                    63

                    Which province opposed the One Unit Scheme in 1955?

                    Correct Answer: Sindh
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The One Unit was the reorganization of the provinces of Pakistan .

                    Prime Minister Muhammad Ali Bogra led it on 22 November 1954.

                    and passed on 30 September 1955

                    This program merged the four provinces of West Pakistan into a single province to parallel the province of East Pakistan

                    • West Punjab
                    • Sind
                    • NWFP
                    • Baluchistan

                    Additional information:

                    The One Unit scheme was dissolved on July 1, 1970

                    General Yahya Khan imposed Legal Framework Order No. 1970 to end the program

                    and reinstate the provisional status of the Four Provinces as of August 1947.

                    64

                    Which glacier feeds the Hunza River?

                    Correct Answer: Batura Glacier
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Batura Glacier is the main source of the Hunza River in Gilgit-Baltistan.

                    It is one of the largest glaciers outside the polar regions.

                    65

                    Which of the following pass connects Sindh Plain with Quetta?

                    Correct Answer: Bolan Pass
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Important Passes of Pakistan

                    1. Khyber Pass 

                    connects: Peshawar to Kabul

                    connects: Pakistan to Afghanistan

                    Height 1070 meters, 3510 feet 


                    2. Bolan Pass 

                    Connects: JCB and Sibi with Quetta 

                    Height: 1793 meters, 5884 feet 


                    3. Lowari Pass

                    Connects: Chitral with Dir 

                    Height: 3118 meters, 10230 feet 


                    4. Broghil Pass 

                    Location: Pakistan & Afghanistan border 

                    Height: 3798 meters, 12460 feet 


                    5. Malakand Pass 

                    Connects: Dir with Chitral 

                    Height: 1362 meters, 4468 feet 


                    6. Shandur pass

                    Connects: Gupis/Gilgit with Chitral 

                    Height: 3700 meters, 12139 feet 


                    7. Khanjerab Pass (top most in Pakistan) 

                    Other name: Friendship Gate

                    Connects: China with Pakistan 

                    Height: 4693 meters, 15397 feet 


                    8. Dorah pass

                    Connects: Badakhshan province of Afghanistan with Chitral 

                    Height: 4300 meters, 14108 feet 


                    9. Babusar Pass

                    Connects: Mansehra/Abbotabad with Gilgit


                    10. Kohat Pass

                    Connects: Kohat with Peshawar 


                    11. Khojak Pass

                    Connects: Qilla Abdullah with Chaman


                    12. Tochi Pass

                    Connects: Ghazni with Bannu

                    66

                    Which sector of Pakistan pays the most taxes?

                    Correct Answer: Salaried class
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The salaried class contributes most to income tax through payroll deductions.

                    Other sectors like agriculture and small businesses often have lower tax compliance.

                    67

                    The Security Council passed its first Resolution on Kashmir on ____?

                    Correct Answer: January 17, 1948
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • The security council passed its first resolution on Kashmir on January 17, 1948.
                    • ***
                    •  United Nations Security Council Resolution 39, adopted on January 20, 1948.
                    • The United Nations Security Council Resolution 47, adopted on 21 April 1948, concerns the resolution of the Kashmir conflict.
                    68

                    The First Muslim ruler to introduce the System of Price Control in the sub-continent was ______?

                    Correct Answer: Allau ud din Khilji
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The system of market control or price control was introduced by Alauddin Khalji during the Sultanate period.

                    He was the second ruler of the Khilji Dynasty in India



                    AGM 24-12-2022


                    69

                    The Upper Indus Plain ends at _____?

                    Correct Answer: Mithankot
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Indus River starts in Tibet

                    The river Indus originates from the Mansarovar lake

                    The Indus River flows through India, Pakistan,

                    The Indus River finally falls into the Arabian Sea near Karachi.

                    River Indus starts making a delta near the city of Thatta

                    The old name of the Indus river was: Sindhu

                    National River of Pakistan: Indus River

                    6 Barrages constructed in Indus River

                    Indus River Length: 3,180 km

                    Indus River 2nd source: Gar Tsangpo

                    The upper Indus plain ends at Mithankot 

                    The upper Indus plain starts from Kalabagh

                    70

                    Which City of Pakistan is famous for sports goods?

                    Correct Answer: Sialkot
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    Sialkot is a city located in Punjab, Pakistan.
                    It is the capital of the Sialkot District
                    Sialkot is the 13th most populous city in Pakistan
                    Sialkot is located in the north-east Punjab
                    Sialkot is one of Pakistan's most industrialized regions.
                    The postal code of Sialkot is  51310
                    The calling code of Sialkot is 052
                    Sialkot is known for manufacturing and export of many items:

                    such as
                    1. surgical instruments
                    2. musical instruments
                    3. sports goods
                    4. leather goods
                    5. textile goods 

                    71

                    When National Bank of Pakistan established?

                    Correct Answer: 1949
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    National Bank was created on November 9, 1949, under National Bank of Pakistan Ordinance, 1949 for the purpose.

                    The NBP has over 1,500 branches in Pakistan and more than 18 locations overseas.

                    72

                    When State Bank of Pakistan (SBP) was inaugurated?

                    Correct Answer: 1st July 1948
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The State Bank of Pakistan was inaugurated on 1st July 1948.

                    The headquarters of the State Bank of Pakistan in Karachi Sindh.

                    History of State Bank

                    Zahid Husain was the founder and the first governor of the SBP from June 1948 to July 1953.

                    Dr. Shamshad Akhtar was the first female Governor of the SBP in 2006.

                    Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah inaugurated the State Bank of Pakistan.

                    The Governor is appointed by the President of Pakistan.

                    Term of five years.

                    73

                    Pakistan’s first economic budget was presented in which year?

                    Correct Answer: 1948
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Pakistan’s First Budget – Key Points

                    Presented in: February 1948

                    Finance Minister: Malik Ghulam Muhammad

                    Significance: Marked the start of Pakistan’s financial planning after independence

                    Outcome: Led to the conceptualization of the first Five-Year Plan around 1950 (implemented later)

                    74

                    Where Jhelum River starts?

                    Correct Answer: Held Kashmir
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Jhelum River originates from a deep spring at Vernag in western Jammu and Kashmir.

                    The river flows northwest from the Pir Panjal Range through the Vale of Kashmir.

                    It empties into Wular Lake at Srinagar, which regulates its flow.

                    75

                    Which province of Pakistan has the largest number of divisions?

                    Correct Answer: Punjab
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    There are total 37 Divisions in Pakistan.

                    Punjab: 10 Divisions

                    Balochistan: 08 Divisions

                    KPK: 07 Divisions

                    Sindh: 06 Divisions

                    Gilgit-Baltistan: 03 Divisions

                    Azad Kashmir: 03 Divisions


                    There are total 170 Districts in Pakistan.

                    Punjab: 42 Districts

                    KPK: 38 Districts

                    Balochistan: 36 Districts

                    Sindh: 30 Districts

                    Gilgit-Baltistan: 14 Districts

                    Azad Kashmir: 10 Districts

                    76

                    When did Pakistan officially recognize/accept the Government of Bangladesh?

                    Correct Answer: February 1974
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • Pakistan officially recognized the Government of Bangladesh on February 22, 1974
                    • During the 2nd OIC meeting in Lahore.
                    • Prime Minister Zulfikar Ali Bhutto announced the recognition
                    • Pressure from Arab leaders to invite Bangladesh's Sheikh Mujibur Rehman to the conference

                    Additional information 

                    List of countries in order of their recognition of Bangladesh on 22 February 1974

                    • Malagasy: 14 April 1972
                    • Iraq 18 July 1972
                    • Upper Volta: 19 September 1972
                    • Pakistan: 22 February 1974

                    ****

                    Last updated by NS on 01 MAY 2026

                    77

                    Kalash Valley is situated in ______?

                    Correct Answer: Chitral
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Kalash Valleys are located in Chitral District in northern (Khyber Pakhtunkhwa) Pakistan.

                    More specifically, they are surrounded by the Hindu Kush mountain range.

                    It is home to the Kalash people, known for their unique culture, language, and festivals.

                    78

                    The ruins of Harappa are situated in which district?

                    Correct Answer: Sahiwal
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Harappa, village in eastern Punjab province, eastern Pakistan

                    The ruins of Harappa are situated in   Sahiwal District, Punjab, Pakistan

                    The Bronze Age Harappan civilization, now more often called the Indus Valley Civilisation

                    Harappa is small crossroads town of 15,000 people today.
                    79

                    Ch. Rehmat Ali graduated from which institution?

                    Correct Answer: Islamia College Lahore
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Ch. Rehmat Ali graduated from Islamia College Lahore.

                    It was affiliated with the University of Punjab at the time.

                    After obtaining his initial degree, he pursued further studies at the University of Cambridge.

                    It was called to the Bar at Middle Temple Inn in London. 

                    80

                    Manchar Lake receives water mainly from which river?

                    Correct Answer: Indus
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Manchhar Lake is the largest natural freshwater lake in Jamshoro Sindh Pakistan.

                    It collects water from the Kithar mountain range.

                    It is one of South Asia's largest.

                    Manchhar Lake is the largest natural freshwater lake in Pakistan.

                    Manchhar Lake covers an area of  23.5 km (14.6 mi).

                    It is part of Indus River basin.

                    81

                    The first Martial Law was imposed on Pakistan under leadership of ____?

                    Correct Answer: General Ayub Khan
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Ø  Iskandar Ali Mirza who was the first president of Pakistan abrogated the constitution of 1956 on 07-October-1958.

                    Ø  Ayoub Khan was made chief martial law after this abrogation.

                    Ø  Feroz Khan Noun was the prime minister at the time of the first Martial Law in Pakistan.

                    ******

                    List of Martial Laws Held in Pakistan from 1947 to 2022

                    Sr#

                     Martial Law Number

                    Date of Martial Law

                    Martial Law Implemented by

                    1

                    First Martial Law

                    7th October 1958

                    Iskandar Mirza

                    2

                    2nd Martial Law

                    25th March 1969

                    General Yahya Khan

                    3

                    3rd Martial Law

                    25th July 1977

                    General Zia ul Haq

                    4

                    4th Martial Law

                    12th October 1999

                    General Pervaiz Musharraf

                     

                    There are total four martial laws Implemented in Pakistan from 1947 to 2022

                    82

                    Female Inspector of which province named Irum Khanam won two gold medals in world powerlifting Championship 2025?

                    Correct Answer: Punjab
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Pakistani (Punjab) police female Inspector Irum Khanum won two gold medals at world powerlifting Championship 2025.

                    She secured first position in both the powerlifting and deadlift events, defeating Indian competitor Sapna to claim the titles.

                    She belongs to Rawalpindi Police. 


                    Additional Information:

                    The event was held in Colombo, Sri Lanka. The competition ran from November 27 to 30, 2025.

                    ***

                    Name the Pakistani police female inspector who won gold medal in world powerlifting Championship 2025?

                    Correct Answer: Irum Khanum
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Pakistani (Punjab) police female Inspector Irum Khanum won two gold medals at world powerlifting Championship 2025.

                    She secured first position in both the powerlifting and deadlift events, defeating Indian competitor Sapna to claim the titles.

                    She belongs to Rawalpindi Police. 


                    Additional Information:

                    The event was held in Colombo, Sri Lanka. The competition ran from November 27 to 30, 2025.

                    ***

                    84

                    Where has World’s largest markhor sculpture been unveiled?

                    Correct Answer: Kaghan Valley
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    In December 2025, Kaghan Valley became home to the world’s largest sculpture of the Markhor, Pakistan’s national animal, installed near the Shah Dawood Palace site in the Batakundi area.

                    The massive structure took five years to complete.

                    Height: 105 feet high and 38 feet wide

                    ****

                    85

                    Who has become the first ISI chief in Pakistan to be court martialed?

                    Correct Answer: Lieutenant General (r) Faiz Hameed
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    On 11 Dec 2025, Former chief of Inter-Services Intelligence (ISI) Faiz Hameed was sentenced to 14 years imprisonment by a military court.

                    He was sentenced on charges including violation of state secrets and interfering in politics.

                    Note: It is the first time that an ISI chief in Pakistan has been court martialled.

                     

                    Additional info:

                    Faiz Hameed remained chief of ISI from 2019 to 2021.

                     ***

                    86

                    River Ravi rises in the South East of Pir Punjal range and joins the River Chenab. Its length is ____ kms.

                    Correct Answer: 765
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Ravi River is a transboundary river crossing northwestern India and eastern Pakistan.

                    Ravi River length: 765 km

                    Khalafat movement was abolished in which year?

                    Correct Answer: 1924
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Khilafat movement start in 1919-

                    The Khilafat movement ended in 1924

                    Khalafat movement started in India during world war 1 

                    For the purposes to protect the khalafat

                    The Ottoman Caliphate,  was abolished on 3 March 1924  by decree of the Grand National Assembly of Turkey.

                    The process was one of Atatürk's Reforms following the replacement of the Ottoman Empire with the Republic of Turkey.

                    Derawar Fort is located in ______?

                    Correct Answer: Bahawalpur
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    It is located in Cholistan Desert, Ahmadpur East Tehsil, Bahawalpur.

                    The walls have a perimeter of 1500 metres and stand up to 30 metres high.

                    90

                    MPA stands for _________?

                    Correct Answer: Member of Provincial Assembly
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    A Member of the Provincial Assembly or MPA, is a representative elected by the voters of an electoral district to the legislature or legislative assembly of a subnational jurisdiction.

                    91

                    How many rivers flow in Sindh Province?

                    Correct Answer: 4
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Indus River: The principal river, flowing through the entire province, vital for irrigation and life. 

                    Malir River: Flows through Karachi, often considered more of a drain now. 

                    Lyari River: Another Karachi river, also largely a drain. 

                    Hub River: Forms part of the Sindh-Balochistan border.

                    Correct Answer: 1979

                    Jawaharlal Nehru

                    Sukarno

                    Josip Broz Tito

                    Kwame Nkrumah

                    Gamal Abdel Nasse

                    Height of Islamabad from sea level is _________?

                    Correct Answer: 1700 feet
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    At the foot of the Margalla Hills
                    Its elevation is 540 meters (1,770 ft)

                    Additional information:
                    Islamabad officially became the capital of Pakistan on 14 August 1967.
                    It is the country's ninth-most populous city
                    With a population of over 1.2 million people
                    It was Constructed in 1960

                    Fatima Jinnah was a presidential candidate against Ayub Khan

                    95

                    Quaid-e-Azam was sworn in as the first Governor General of Pakistan on?

                    Correct Answer: 15 August 1947
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • The oath of Governor-General administered to Quiad-e-Azam by Justice Abdul Rasheed
                    • ***
                    • Sir Mian Abdul Rashid,was the first Chief Justice of Pakistan.
                    • On 15 August 1947, when Quaid-i-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah was sworn in as the first Governor-General of Pakistan, Rashid, as the most senior Muslim judge in British India, administered the oath of office to him.

                    1st Governor-General of Pakistan 

                    Quaid e Azam born on: 25 December 1876 in Karachi

                    Died on: 11 September 1948

                    In-office: 14 August 1947 – 11 September 1948

                    Prime Minister Liaquat Ali Khan

                    Succeeded by Khawaja Nazimuddin

                    96

                    Baran river is flowing in the province of?

                    Correct Answer: Sindh
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Baran river, also known as the Nai Baran river or Baran Nai,

                    It is situated in the Sindh province of Pakistan

                    A hill torrent that ranges from the Sindh to the Balochistan provinces of Pakistan, at the foot of the Kirthar Mountains range.

                    97

                    Masjid Wazir Khan was made in which century?

                    Correct Answer: 17th
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Wazir Khan Mosque is a 17th-century mosque located in the city of Lahore.

                    The mosque was commissioned during the reign of the Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan.

                    Opened: 1642

                    ***


                    98

                    Which train was attacked by Baloch Liberation Army (BLA) in March 2025 killing more than 20 passengers?

                    Correct Answer: Jaffar Express
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The train named Jaffar Express hijacked by BLA in Balochistan in March 2025.

                    The train was targeted by terrorists in the area of Bolan, Balochistan on March 11, 2025.

                    The banned Baloch Liberation Army (BLA) claimed responsibility for the attack.

                     

                    Additional Information:

                    As per ISPR, all 33 terrorists killed by Security forces.

                    21 passengers and 4 Frontier Corps personnel dead in this incident

                    ***
                    99

                    1965 war started from ______?

                    Correct Answer: 6 September
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The 1965 war between India and Pakistan began on April 6, 1965 with the start of Operation Gibraltar.

                    India retaliated by crossing the international border at Lahore on September 6, 1965 and attacking Lahore and Kasur.

                    The war lasted 17 days.

                    The war ended with a ceasefire declared on September 22, 1965 after diplomatic intervention by the Soviet Union and the United States.

                    100

                    Which party has most seats in Senate?

                    Correct Answer: Pakistan Peoples Party
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Pakistan Peoples Party (PPP) currently holds the most seats in the Pakistani Senate

                    Number of Seats: 26 out of 96 total seats.


                    Political groups

                    *Government (71)

                    •   PPP (26)
                    •   PMLN (19)
                    •   JUI (F) (6)
                    •   BAP (4)
                    •   MQM-P (4)
                    •   ANP (3)
                    •   NP (1)
                    •   PML(Q) (1)
                    •   IND (6)

                    *Opposition (15)

                    •   MWM (1)
                    •   IND (15)
                    101

                    Rakaposhi is also known as Dumani, which means?

                    Correct Answer: Mother of Mist
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Rakaposhi is also known as Dumani, meaning "Mother of Mist" or "Mother of Clouds."

                    Additional information 


                    Rakaposhi is the 27th-highest mountain in the world.

                    It is the steepest peak in the world.

                    It is located in the Gilgit-Baltistan region of Pakistan.

                    Rakaposhi rises over the Nagar Valley.

                    It is the part of the Karakoram range.

                    It is the 12th highest mountain in Pakistan.

                    The first successful ascent was in 1958 by Mike Banks and Tom Patey.

                    102

                    The 'Bloodless Revolution' or 'Glorious Revolution' took place in which year?

                    Correct Answer: 1688
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Glorious Revolution (or Bloodless Revolution) took place in 1688 in England

                    Key Figures: James II (deposed), William of Orange (William III), and Mary II (his wife).

                    Significance: Established that the monarch's power was limited by Parliament, laying the groundwork for modern democracy. 

                    103

                    Indian National Congress formed in 1885. Who was the Viceroy at that time?

                    Correct Answer: Lord Dufferin
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • The Viceroy of India at the time the Indian National Congress was formed in 1885 was Lord Dufferin.
                    • The Indian National Congress was formed when 72 representatives from all over the country met at Bombay in 1885.
                    • The Indian National Congress dominated the Indian movement for independence from Great Britain.
                    104

                    In 1942, Muslim League did not take part in _____?

                    Correct Answer: Quit India Movement
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Muslim League did not support the Quit India Movement because it was aimed at both Muslims and then British. Instead, the League demanded "Divide and Quit".

                    The Muslim League, the Communist Party of India, and the Hindu Mahasabha did not support the movement.

                    105

                    FATA was merged with KPK under which constitutional amendment?

                    Correct Answer: 25th
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    FATA was merged with KPK in May 2018.

                    FATA stands for Federally Administered Tribal Areas

                    FATA was merged with KPK under 25th amendment. 

                    After the merger of the Federally Administered Tribal Areas with KPK,

                    FATA seats were abolished and there will be no election on 4 of the 8 seats.

                    The rest of the 4 seats will also not be renewed in 2024.

                    *********

                    106

                    The Election of Chief Minister shall be held by:

                    Correct Answer: Open Ballot
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The election of the Chief Minister shall be held by the members of the legislative assembly or any other specified body,

                    depending on the electoral process outlined in the constitution or relevant legislation.

                    107

                    As per Article 10A of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973, for the determination of his _______ obligations or in any criminal charge against him a person shall be entitled to a fair and due process.

                    Correct Answer: Civil rights
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Article 10A of the Constitution of Pakistan 1973 ensures the right to fair trial and due process.

                    It applies in the determination of a person’s civil rights and obligations or in any criminal charge against him.

                    108

                    According to the 1956 Constitution, who could be the head of state of Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: A Muslim only
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    According to the 1956 Constitution of Pakistan, only a Muslim could be elected as the President (Head of State).

                    This was the first constitution of Pakistan and it officially declared Pakistan as an Islamic Republic.

                    109

                    Article 25 of the 1973 Constitution of Pakistan deals with?

                    Correct Answer: Equality of citizens
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Article 25 of the 1973 Constitution of Pakistan deals with Equality of citizens.

                    Article 25(A) of constitution of 1973 deals with Right to education.

                    110

                    Any right which has been provided by the Constitution of 1973 is called —— ?

                    Correct Answer: Fundamental right
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Any right specifically provided for and guaranteed by the Constitution of 1973 is called a Fundamental right.

                    These rights are considered essential for the comprehensive development of citizens and are legally enforceable through the high courts and Supreme Court of Pakistan. 

                    111

                    The parliament of Pakistan consists of______?

                    Correct Answer: National assembly, senate and president
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • The Upper House of Parliament of Pakistan is Senate
                    • ****
                    • The Parliament of Pakistan is bicameral.
                    • Article 50 of the Constitution states that the Parliament of Pakistan consists of the President and Prime Minister
                    • two Houses are known as
                      •             the National Assembly (lower house)
                      •             the Senate. (upper house)
                    • ****

                    • agm.04.03.23


                    112

                    Which constitutional amendment restored parliamentary democracy after Gen Zia's Marshal law?

                    Correct Answer: 8th
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Eighteenth Amendment to Pakistan's constitution 

                     Passed in 2010

                     It restored parliamentary democracy by reducing presidential powers.

                    It reversed many changes made during military rule, especially under General Zia-ul-Haq.

                    113

                    Which Pakistani University was ranked among the top 100 in Asia in 2025?

                    Correct Answer: Quad-i-Azam
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    In the QS World University Rankings: Asia 2025 were ranked in the top 100

                    National University of Sciences and Technology (NUST)  at 67th 

                    Quaid-i-Azam University (QAU) at 84th



                    114

                    First English Music channel of Radio Pakistan is based in _____?

                    Correct Answer: Islamabad
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    On 14 November, PBC launched its first English Music Channel in Islamabad called Planet 94.

                    The network operates on FM 94.

                    115

                    Which of the following Cash Crop of Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: Cotton
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The five major crops in Pakistan are:

                    1. Cotton
                    2. Wheat
                    3. Rice
                    4. Sugarcane
                    5. Maize

                    Cotton is the most important cash crop.

                    It followed by rice, which is the second main staple food crop and a major export commodity.

                    Cash crop means نقدی فصل

                    116

                    Which city is called ‘Brasilia of Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: Islamabad
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Islamabad is called the Brazil of Pakistan

                    • because Islamabad is the capital of Pakistan
                    • and Islamabad is a very neat and clean city

                    It has been the capital of Pakistan since 1967.

                    It is the country's ninth-most populous city,



                    117

                    The Quit India Movement started at:

                    Correct Answer: Bombay on Aug 8, 1942

                    Started: Aug 8, 1942

                    Started from: Bombay 

                    Launched by: Gandhi

                    Also known as: August Movement 

                    Demand: to end British rule

                    Jinnah changed the slogan "Divide and Quit India" 

                    Started by party: Congress

                    118

                    Rawal Dam is built on the river _____?

                    Correct Answer: Korang
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                  • Rawal Dam was built in 1962 by the Punjab Government.
                  • It supplies water to Rawalpindi and Islamabad.
                  • The dam is constructed on the Korang River.
                  • The Korang River originates in the Murree Hills.
                  • It flows toward Islamabad.
                    • 119

                    The biggest library in Pakistan is?

                    Correct Answer: Quaid-e-Azam Library
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Quaid-e-Azam Library is a public library located within the Bagh-e-Jinnah in Lahore, Punjab, Pakistan.

                    The library was constructed in the mid-19th century during the British Raj compromises of Victorian era Lawrence and Montgomery Halls. 

                    Established: 1886

                    The complex includes two halls, the first was built in memory of John Laird Mair Lawrence, 1st Baron Lawrence,

                    and the second in memory of Robert Montgomery Martin

                    1 Mr. Liaqat Ali Khan 15-08-1947 To 16-10-1951

                    Jamrud Fort is located near ________ ?

                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    122

                    What is the total area of Sindh Province?

                    Correct Answer: 140,914 Sq Km
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Sindh is a province of Pakistan.

                    Sindh is the third-largest province of Pakistan by land area

                    Sindh is the second-largest province by population after Punjab

                    Sindh is known as Bab-ul-Islam (the gateway of Islam),


                    Sindh has 30 Districts and 7 7 administrative divisions

                    The mountain range located in Sindh is the Kirthar Range

                    There are three Barrages in Sindh

                    The mountain range located in Sindh is the Kirthar Range

                    Sindhi is written in a version of the Arabic script.

                    Sindhi is the official language of the Pakistani province of Sindh.

                    The Sindhi language is the oldest language

                    Karachi is the biggest Division of Sindh by population and Hyderabad is the largest Division of Sindh by area.

                    What is the total estimated forest area of Sindh ?

                    0.92 million hectares

                    Who was the viceroy of India at the time of architect of partition of Bengal?

                    Correct Answer: Lord Curzon
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • The Partition of Bengal was announced by Lord Curzon on 20 July 1905.
                      • It came into force on 16 October 1905.
                      • The Hindu community and the Indian National Congress strongly opposed it.
                      • In December 1911, King George V announced the revocation of the partition at his Coronation Durbar in Delhi.

                      Pakistan's first Nobel Prize winner, Professor Dr. Abdul Salam, got his Master's in Mathematics from _____?

                      Correct Answer: Government College, Lahore
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Professor Dr. Abdul Salam, Pakistan's first Nobel Prize winner, received his Master's degree in Mathematics from Government College, Lahore in 1946.

                      He later went on to earn his PhD in Physics from Cambridge University.

                      He was awarded the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1979 for his work on the electroweak unification of the fundamental forces of nature.
