                      140

                      When did Simon commission arrive in India?

                      Correct Answer: 3rd February 1928
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      ·        The Indian Statutory Commission also known as Simon Commission,

                      ·        was a group of seven Members of Parliament

                      ·        under the chairmanship of Sir John Simon.

                      ·        The commission arrived in India in 1928

                      ·        to study constitutional reform in Britain's largest and most important possession.

                      Simon Commission wanted to check the working of Government of India Act, 1919.

                      141

                      Gomal Pass is located near the city of:

                      Correct Answer: Zhob
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Gomal Pass is located near the city of zhob valley

                      Gomal Pass is a mountain pass on the Durand Line border between Afghanistan and the southeastern portion of South Waziristan in Pakistan's KPK 

                      It takes its name from the Gomal River and is midway between the legendary Khyber Pass and the Bolan Pass.

                      It connects Ghazni in Afghanistan with Tank and Dera Ismail Khan in Pakistan.

                      142

                      Agha Khan University was established in ______?

                      Correct Answer: 1983
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      AKU was Founded in 1983 as Pakistan's first private university.

                      The Aga Khan University is an international.

                      It was founded by Prince Aga Khan IV the university expanded to:

                      Kenya

                      Tanzania

                      Uganda

                      the United Kingdom

                      Afghanistan.

                      143

                      KANUPP is located near ?

                      Correct Answer: Baleji Beach
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      KANUPP stands for Karachi Nuclear Power Plant

                      it is a large commercial nuclear power plant located at Paradise Point in Karachi Sindh 

                      it is Pakistan's first nuclear energy plant

                      it is composed of three commercial nuclear power plants

                      K1: 1 August 1966 (support from Canada) 

                      K2: 20 August 2015 (investment provided by China and IAEA)

                      K3: 31 May 2016 (investment provided by China and IAEA)

                      144

                      PEPA stands for _____?

                      Correct Answer: Pakistan Environmental Protection Agency
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      PEPA stands for Pakistan Environmental Protection Agency.

                      It is a governmental organization responsible for environmental protection and conservation in Pakistan.

                      Headquarters (HQ): Islamabad, Pakistan

                      Founded in: 1997 (under the Pakistan Environmental Protection Act, 1997)

                      145

                      PASSCO stands for?

                      Correct Answer: Pakistan Agriculture Seed Supplies Corporation
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Pakistan Agriculture Seed Supplies Corporation is a Pakistani government-owned company working in the storage sector of Pakistan, under administrative control of Ministry of National Food Security and Research, Govt. of Pakistan.

                      146

                      PATA stands for_____?

                      Correct Answer: Provincially Administered Tribal Areas
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      PATA stands for Provincially Administrate Tribal Areas

                      It was the former administrative subdivision of Pakistan designated in Article 246(b) of the Constitution of Pakistan.

                      147

                      Basic Democracy system came in ______?

                      Correct Answer: May 1959
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      After the imposition of Martial Law in 1958,

                      General Muhammad Ayub Khan introduced the concept of basic democracy through the Basic Democracy Order, in 1959.

                      On 26 October, Ayub Khan announces his system of 80,000 Basic Democrats

                      The voting was to be indirect, as the President had to be elected by the 80,000 "basic democrats", who made up the Electoral College.

                      The electoral system was made indirect, and the `Basic Democrats', for both wings were declared Electoral College for the purpose of electing the Assemblies and the President.

                      (40,000 from each East & West Pakistan).

                      148

                      Pakistan’s Standard time was adopted on _________?

                      Correct Answer: September 1951
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      • Pakistan had been following UTC+05:30 since 1907
                      • continued using it after independence in 1947
                      • ***

                      • Pakistan's standard time was suggested by Professor Muhammad Anwar in 1951
                      • **
                      • On 15 September 1951, Mathematician Mahmood Anwar, two time zones were introduced.
                      • Pakistan's standard time was adopted on 15 September 1951.


                      149

                      Who was the founder of a college known as Madrassah-e-Rahimyah?

                      Correct Answer: Shah Abdur Rahim
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      The founder of Madrassah-e-Rahimyah was Shah Abdur Rahim.

                      **More Information's***

                      It was founded by Shah Abdur Rahim, the father of Shah Waliullah Dehlawi,

                      During the reign of Mughal Emperor Aurangzeb.

                      After the death of Shah Abdur Rahim in 1718 Shah Waliullah started teaching at the Madrasah.

                      ****

                      Type: Islamic Theology

                      Established: 18th century

                      Affiliation: Naqshbandi Sufism, Hanafi school

                      Location: Delhi, India


                      150

                      Who founded Urdu Defence Association?

                      Correct Answer: Mohsin-ul-Mulk
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      The Urdu Defence Association was an organization developed by Mohsin-ul-Mulk


                      The Urdu Defence Association was started in April 1900


                      The Urdu Defence Association started the advocacy for Urdu as the lingua franca of the Muslim community of India. 



                      The association is regarded as an offshoot of the Aligarh Movement.

                      ***

                      Nawab Mohsin-ul-Mulk, Munir Nawaz Jang, also known as Syed Mehdi Ali


                      Nawab Mohsin-ul-Mulk was an Indian Muslim politician.


                      Nawab Mohsin-﻿ul-Mulkwas involved in the Aligarh Movement


                      Nawab Mohsin-ul-Mulk was one of the founders of the All-India Muslim League in 1906.

                      What was the Real Name of Nawab Mohsin-ul-Mulk?

                      Correct Answer: Syed Mehdi Ali
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Nawab Mohsin-ul-Mulk, was an Indian Muslim politician.

                      Other names: Syed Mehdi Ali, Munir Nawaz Jang

                      He was involved in the Aligarh Movement and was one of the founders of the All-India Muslim League in 1906.

                      Born: December 9, 1837, Etawah, India

                      Died: October 16, 1907, Shimla, India

                      Party: All-India Muslim League


                      154

                      Ranking of Thar desert in the world (area wise) is?

                      Correct Answer: 9th
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      It is the world's 18th-largest desert and the world's 9th-largest hot subtropical desert.



                      About Thar Desert

                      15% located in Pakistan

                      85% located in India

                      Also called Friendly Desert

                      Located near Badin, Sindh 

                      Famous for: Sand 

                      Coal also found in Thar 

                      also known as Great Indian Desert

                      Area: 77,000 Miles

                      What is Father name of Allama Iqbal?

                      Correct Answer: Sheikh Noor Muhammad
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Allama Iqbal Father was Sheikh Noor Muhammad

                      He was a tailor

                      Allama Iqbal Mother name is Imam Bibi


                       Additional Information

                      Allama Iqbal Born: November 9, 1877 (Sialkot)

                      He was Died: 21 April 1938

                      His Burial: Badshahi Mosque in Lahore

                      They Received PhD: in Germany

                      His Title: Poet of the East

                      Punjab was a political area of Allama Iqbal

                      He was elected to the Punjab Legislative Council in 1927



                      156

                      How many census have done in Pakistan?

                      Correct Answer: 7
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      There are the Total 7 Census held in Pakistan:

                      1. 1951: The first census after Pakistan's independence.
                      2. 1961: The second census.
                      3. 1972: The third census, delayed due to the 1971 war.
                      4. 1981: The fourth census.
                      5. 1998: The fifth census.
                      6. 2017: The sixth census.
                      7. 2023: The 7th population and housing census was conducted digitally.
                      157

                      What was Mother tongue of Quaid-e-Azam?

                      Correct Answer: Gujrati
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah's mother tongue was Gujarati.

                      His family migrated to Karachi from the Kathiawar region of Gujarat, and they spoke Gujarati at home. 

                      158

                      Which is the Largest Division in Pakistan in terms of area?

                      Correct Answer: Kalat Division
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Qalat is the largest division with regard to area.

                      Lahore is the largest division of Pakistan with regard to population.

                      159

                      Dr. Abdus Salam, the Nobel Prize winner of Pakistan, belonged to ______?

                      Correct Answer: Punjab
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Dr. Abdus Salam was Born in Jhang Punjab: Jan. 29, 1926

                      Dr. Abdus Salam was Died: Nov. 21, 1996

                      In 1979,  Dr. Abdul Salam won the Nobel Prize for physics.

                      Dr. Abdul Salam received Nobel Prize for combining two forces: Electromagnetic and weak forces

                      He was the second Muslim scientist to get Nobel Prize.

                      Abdus Salam became the first Pakistani to win a Nobel Prize.

                      In 1952 he Ph.D. in theoretical physics from the University of Cambridge.

                      160

                      Which was the first public airline of Pakistan?

                      Correct Answer: Orient airline
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Orient airline was the first and only Muslim-owned airline.

                      Orient airline was established on 29 October 1946.

                      Orient Airlines was initially based in Calcutta, India.

                      Orient airline shifted to the independent state of Pakistan in 1947.

                      Orient airline was rechristened as Pakistan International Airlines in 1955

                      Orient airline is the old name of PIA airlines.

                      161

                      CSP stands for?

                      Correct Answer: Central Superior Services
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      The Central Superior Services (CSS)

                      It is responsible for running the bureaucratic operations and government secretariats and directorates of the Cabinet of Pakistan.

                      Formation: 26 September 1973

                      162

                      Makli Hill Graveyard is located in which city?

                      Correct Answer: Thatta
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Makli Necropolis was inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1981.

                      Makli Necropolis, located in Thatta, Pakistan.

                      It is one of the largest funerary sites in the world.

                      It is the final resting place of some 125,000 local rulers, Sufi saints, and other eminent persons from the 14th to the 18th century.

                      163

                      The next senior rank to Sub Inspector in Police is SHO/Inspector. What does SHO stand for?

                      Correct Answer: Station House Officer
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      SHO stands for Station House Officer. 

                       The SHO holds the rank of inspector or sub-inspector.

                      The rule of SHO in Pakistan is the overall supervision of the functions of each police station

                      SHO is the head or officer in charge of a police station of a locality.

                      164

                      What was the distance between East and West Pakistan?

                      Correct Answer: 1600 miles
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      The distance between East and West Pakistan was approximately 1600 miles.

                      India lay in between, separating the two wings.
                      165

                      What is the height of Minar e Pakistan?

                      Correct Answer: 70 m
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Height: 70 m

                      Architect: Nasreddin Murat-Khan

                      Construction started: March 23, 1960

                      Location: Lahore, Punjab; Pakistan

                      Opened: October 21, 1968

                      Architectural styles: Modern architecture, Mughal architecture


                      166

                      Gomal Zam Dam is in which province?

                      Correct Answer: KPK
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Gomal Zam Dam is a gravity dam in South Waziristan Tribal District of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa.

                      The dam impounds the Gomal River.

                      167

                      Khewara mines are famous for:

                      Correct Answer: Sodium Chloride deposits
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      The Khewra Salt Mine is in Khewra, north of Pind Dadan Khan, in Jhelum District, Punjab Region, Pakistan.

                      The mine is in the Salt Range, Potohar plateau, which rises from the Indo-Gangetic Plain.

                      It is the second largest in the world

                      168

                      What is the length of the silk highway route in Pakistan?

                      Correct Answer: 887 KM
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      The Silk Route is also known as Karakoram Highway

                      The silk road connects Pakistan and China.

                      The total length is approximately 1,300 kilometers, of which 887 kilometers are in Pakistan and 413 km in China.

                      The silk road is considered a symbol of Pakistan-China's eternal friendship

                      The highway meets the Pakistani-Chinese border at Khunjerab Pass

                       It was started in 1959 and was completed and opened to the public in 1979.

                      Karakorum Highway is located in the Karakoram range

                      Karakorum highway in Pakistan starts from Hassan Abdal

                      Karakorum highway is also called N-35

                      ****

                      ND9-11-2022

                      173

                      Bala Hisar Fort is located in _______?

                      Correct Answer: Peshawar
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Babur first built a fort here in 1526 after capturing Peshawar.

                      First mentioned by 7th-century explorer Xuanzang, the fort was used as a royal residence for the Durrani Empire since December 1747.

                      The Maratha Empire captured it after the Battle of Peshawar in 1758.

                      The Sikhs reconstructed the fort after capturing Peshawar in March 1823.

                      174

                      The first invasion of Mahmud Ghaznvi was in ______?

                      Correct Answer: 1000 AD
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      The first invasion of Mahmoon Ghaznavi against India was in 1000 AD. 

                      Mahmood Ghazni is known as the “Abductor of Scholars”.

                      17th attack of Sultan Mehmood Ghazni on India was took place in 1027 AD.

                      175

                      Who made Islamabad the capital of Pakistan______?

                      Correct Answer: Ayoub Khan
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Islamabad officially became the capital of Pakistan on 14 August 1967.

                      Ayoub Khan made Islamabad the capital of Pakistan

                      The area of Islamabad before its declaration as the capital of Pakistan was known as Raj Shahi.

                      Islamabad is the country's ninth-most populous city, with a population of over 1.2 million people

                      Constructed 1960

                      Postcode 44000

                      176

                      Who is the first lady fighter pilot of Pakistan?

                      Correct Answer: Ayesha Farooq

                      Explanation

                      She achieved this milestone in 2013.

                      She becoming a symbol of women's empowerment in the Pakistan Air Force.

                      177

                      The first shaheed female pilot of Pakistan Air Force is ______?

                      Correct Answer: Marium Mukhtiar
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Flying Officer Marium Mukhtiar, born on May 18, 1992.

                      She was a Pakistani fighter pilot who died on November 24, 2015, when her PAF FT-7PG aircraft crashed near Kundian, Mianwali.

                      She was the first female combat pilot of Pakistan to die in a crash on duty.

                      178

                      Who was the first female commercial pilot of Pakistan?

                      Correct Answer: Shukria Khanum
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Shukriya Khanum (c. 1935 – 14 May 2017) was a Pakistani pilot.

                      She was Pakistan’s first woman to obtain a commercial pilot license.

                      She was died of cancer in 2017 at the age of 82 in Lahore.

                      179

                      How many rivers are there in Balochistan____?

                      Correct Answer: 7
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      There are many rivers in Balochistan but 7 are the main

                      The important rivers in Balochistan are:

                      Zhob

                      Nari

                      Bolan

                      Pishin

                      Lora

                      Mula

                      Hub

                      Porali

                      Hingol is the largest river in the Balochistan

                      Rakshan

                      Dasht.

                      ***

                      while

                      24 rivers are present in Pakistan

                      5 rivers flow in Punjab Province

                      4 rivers flow in Sindh Province

                      7 rivers flow in Balochistan Province

                      8 rivers flow in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Province

                      ND16-3-2023





                      180

                      Which city is called the Gateway of Pakistan?

                      Correct Answer: Karachi
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Karachi is called the "Gateway of Pakistan"

                      It has an ideal location and well-developed connections to form gateways region with:

                      • Afghanistan,
                      • Central Asia,
                      • and Western China



                      Additional information:

                      It was the first capital of Pakistan.

                      It is the biggest city in Pakistan.

                      The capital of Karachi is Sindh.


                      181

                      Who was the President of Pakistan during the Indo­ Pak war 1965?

                      Correct Answer: Ayub Khan
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      There are three major Wars (overall 04) fought between India and Pakistan from 1947 to 2021

                       

                      1. The first conflict over the status of the state of Jammu and Kashmir was in 1947.

                      ·         It is also called as first Kashmir war.

                      ·         Result: As a result of the 1947 war, Pakistan gained control of about onr-thirds of the Kashmir region.

                       

                       

                       

                      2. The second conflict between Pakistan and India over the status of the state of Jammu and Kashmir was in 1965.

                      ·         Pakistan's Operation Gibraltar is the main reason for the 1965 war.

                      ·         Ayub Khan was the President of Pakistan during the Indo­ Pak war 1965.

                      ·         Lal Bahadur Shastra was the prime minister of India during the Indo­ Pak war 1965.

                       

                       

                       

                      3. The third conflict between Pakistan and India was in 1971.

                      ·         Yahya Khan was the President of Pakistan during the Indo­ Pak war 1971.

                      ·         Indira Gandhi was the Prime Minister of India during the Indo-Pak war in 1971.

                       

                       

                      4. There is also a limited war in 1999 at Kargil between India and Pakistan.

                       

                      182

                      Who was the first Foreign Minister of Pakistan?

                      Correct Answer: Zafrullah Khan
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      • Sir Muhammad Zafarullah Khan was the first Foreign Minister of Pakistan.
                      • From 27 December 1947 to 24 October 1954
                      • Preceded by Liaquat Ali Khan
                      • Succeeded by Muhammad Ali Bogra
                      • Party Muslim League
                      • The only Pakistani to preside over the International Court of Justice.

                      Last updated question by NS on 11 May 2026

                      Dr. Abdul Salam received Nobel Prize for combining two forces: Electromagnetic and weak forces

                      He was the second Muslim scientist to get Nobel Prize.

                      Abdus Salam became the first Pakistani to win a Nobel Prize.

                      In 1952 he Ph.D. in theoretical physics from the University of Cambridge.

                      After the creation of Pakistan, first newspaper was?

                      Correct Answer: Imroze

                      They were founded by Mian Iftikharuddin in 1947,

                      Originally published in Lahore. 


                      185

                      After how many years Pakistan got its first constitution?

                      Correct Answer: 9 years
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      After 09 years Pakistan got its first constitution.

                      on 23rd March 1956 first constitution of Pakistan was enforced.

                      Choudhry Mohammad Ali was the Prime Minister of Pakistan during the enforcement of the first constitution.

                      There were 234 articles 13 parts and 6 schedules.

                      After the abrogation of the first constitution, the new constitution was enforced by Ayoub Khan on June 1962

                      186

                      East Pakistan was separated from West Pakistan on _______?

                      Correct Answer: 16 December 1971
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      On 16 December 1971, East Pakistan was liberated from Pakistan as the newly independent state of Bangladesh.

                      East Pakistan was the eastern provincial wing of Pakistan between 1947 and 1971.

                      Its land borders were with India and Burma, with a coastline on the Bay of Bengal.

                      India firstly recognized to Bangladesh.

                      The first currency note of SBP was issued on___________?

                      Correct Answer: March 1, 1949

                      Pakistan’s first coin was issued on 3rd January 1948

                      Karachi remained capital of Pakistan from:

                      Correct Answer: 1947 to 1959

                      Rawalpindi remained capital of Pakistan from 1959 to 1967.

                      Islamabad is the current capital of Pakistan since 14 August 1967.

                      190

                      What was the old name of PIA?

                      Correct Answer: Orient Airways
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Orient Airways Ltd. was an airline established in 1946 new name Pakistan International Airlines in 1955

                      The airline shifted operations to the newly independent state of Pakistan in 1947.

                      Pakistan International Airlines is an international airline of Pakistan

                      Headquarters: Karachi

                      Founded: October 29, 1946

                      Founder: Mirza Ahmad Ispahani

                      PIA flight landed in China in 1963

                       PIA, in 1964 became the first non-Communist airline to fly to China

                      ND23-9-2023

                      191

                      What is the second highest peak in Pakistan?

                      Correct Answer: Naga Parbat
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      The second-highest peak in Pakistan is Nanga Parbat,

                      Which stands at 8,126 meters (26,660) feet

                      It is the ninth-highest mountain in the world and is located in the Himalayas.  

                      The meaning of Nanga Parbat is Naked Mountain

                      It is also called Killer Mountain Peak.

                      Location Gilgit–Baltistan, Pakistan

                      Nanga Parbat, known locally as Diamer

                      The mountain range is Nanga Parbat is in the Himalayas, Western Himalaya


                       

                      192

                      What is the other name of Nanga parbat ?

                      Correct Answer: Diamir
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation


                      Nanga Parbat, known locally as Diamer

                      Diamir is a local name for the peak and means “king of the mountains.”


                      The height of Nanga Parbat at 8,126 m (26,660 ft) above sea level.

                      Worldwide Nanga Parbat is the 9th highest mountain.


                      The meaning of Nanga Parbat is Naked Mountain


                      It is also called Killer Mountain Peak.

                       

                      In 1954, the mountain peak of Nanda Parbat was conquered

                       

                      First ascent July 3, 1953

                       

                      Location Gilgit–Baltistan, Pakistan

                       

                      Mountain range is Nanga Parbat is Himalayas, Western Himalaya

                      193

                      Daspang is another name of which mountain?

                      Correct Answer: K-2
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Dapsang is another name for K2

                      Dapsang is the name used by locals in the Balti language. 


                      K2 was given the initial "K" designation because it was the second mountain surveyed in the Karakoram Range

                      K2: The original survey name

                      Chogori: Another local name for K2, which means "king of mountains".

                      Mount Godwin Austen: Named after Henry Godwin-Austen, an English geographer who surveyed the mountain.


                      194

                      Which mountain range is located in Norther areas of Pakistan?

                      Correct Answer: Hamaliya
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      Northern Pakistan is dominated by three major mountain ranges:
                      • The Karakoram
                      • The Himalayas
                      • The Hindu Kush.
                      These ranges converge in this region, which is home to many of the world's highest peaks, including K2. 
                      Karakoram:
                      Located in the northernmost parts of Pakistan, it contains the second-highest peak in the world, K2. 
                      Himalayas:
                      This range runs along the western side of Pakistan and includes the peak Nanga Parbat, the highest in the country. 
                      Hindu Kush:
                      Situated in the northwest of Pakistan, this range extends into Afghanistan and has the peak Tirich Mir as its highest point in the region. 
                      195

                      Which city of Pakistan is called shaheeno ka shehar?

                      Correct Answer: Sargodha
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Shaheenon Ka Shahar means (A city of Eagles)

                      Sargodha is also known as the City of Eagles

                      Sargodha is famous for its orange.

                      Sargodha is Pakistan's 12th-largest city by population

                      Sargodha was founded by the British in 1903

                      Who is the first Women Car mechanic in Pakistan?

                      Pervez Musharraf served as the tenth president of Pakistan from 20 June 2001 – 18 August 2008

                      Pervez Musharraf launched his political party APML in 2010


                      Pervez Musharraf Preceded by: Muhammad Rafiq Tarar


                      Personal detail:

                      Pervez Musharraf Born: 11 August 1943 Delhi, British India

                      Pervez Musharraf Died: 5 February 2023 (aged 79) Dubai, United Arab Emirates

                      Pervez Musharraf died of Amyloidosis disease

                      Pervez Musharraf Resting place: Army Graveyard, Karachi, Pakistan

                      Pervez Musharraf has two child

                      Pervez Musharraf Awarded

                      1. Nishan-e-Imtiaz
                      2. Hilal-i-Imtiaz
                      3. Tamgha-e-Basalat
                      4. Order of King Abdulaziz
                      5. Order of Zayed

                      History:

                      Pervez Musharraf served as the 7th Chief of Army Staff from 1998 to 2007

                      Pervez Musharraf served as the Minister of Defence from October 1999 – 23 October 2002

                      206

                      What form of Government is in place in Pakistan presently?

                      Correct Answer: Parliamentary democracy
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Pakistan currently operates under a parliamentary democracy, where the Prime Minister is the head of government, and the President holds a largely ceremonial role.

                      This system allows elected representatives to govern and make decisions on behalf of the citizens.

                      207

                      Pakistan's first and largest Islamic bank is :

                      Correct Answer: Meezan
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Meezan Bank is Pakistan's first and largest Islamic bank

                      In 2000, Meezan Bank was listed on the Karachi Stock Exchange.


                      Founded: 27 January 1997 by Noor Financial, Pak Kuwait Investment Company, and Islamic Development Bank.

                      Headquarters: Karachi


                      208

                      What us the largest and oldest commercial bank in Pakistan?

                      Correct Answer: HBL
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Habib Bank Limited (HBL) is identified as the largest and oldest commercial bank in Pakistan

                      Habib Bank Limited is a Pakistani multinational bank.

                      HBL was founded in 1940 by the Habib family.

                      HBL became Pakistan's first commercial bank.

                      In 1951 it opened its first international branch in Colombo, Sri Lanka.

                      The Government nationalized the bank in 1974 and privatized it in 2003.

                      In 2003 the Aga Khan Fund for Economic Development acquired a controlling share.

                      HBL major shareholding lies with the Aga Khan Fund for Economic Development (AKFED) which is based in Geneva, Switzerland.

                       

                      As of 2018, HBL has 1700+ branches with presence in over 25 countries spanning four continents.

                      209

                      According to SBP Act 2021, the governor of State Bank of Pakistan (SBP) shall be appointed for a term of________?

                      Correct Answer: Five Years
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      According to SBP Act 2021, the governor of the State Bank of Pakistan (SBP) shall be appointed for a term of five Years

                      ***

                      Mr. Jameel Ahmad assumed the charge of Governor State Bank of Pakistan on August 26, 2022, after his appointment by the President of Pakistan for a period of 5 years.

                      Ms. Kamil is the first woman appointed as Deputy Governor of SBP.

                      Sh1-9-2023


                      210

                      At present Pakistan has vast natural resources and items of mineral as many as________________?

                      Correct Answer: 52
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Mineral resources can be divided into two major categories - Metallic and Nonmetallic.

                      Metallic resources are things like Gold, Silver, Tin, Copper, Lead, Zinc, Iron, Nickel, Chromium, and Aluminum. Nonmetallic resources are things like sand, gravel, gypsum, halite, Uranium, dimension stone

                      211

                      The largest size of iron resources in Pakistan is located at ________?

                      Correct Answer: Kalabagh
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Iron ore is found in various regions of Pakistan including Nokundi, Chiniot, Kalabagh the largest one, Haripur, and other northern areas.

                      The iron ore resources at Kalabagh are 400 million metric tons 

                      while Nokkundi reserves are 50 million tons and Dilband iron ore deposits


                      اس ٹاپک کے متعلق انتہائی اہم سوالات پڑھیں

                      Natural Resources





                      212

                      Which is the number one bank globally?

                      Correct Answer: JP Morgan Chase
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      The number one bank globally depends on the metric used, but based on the provided options, JPMorgan Chase is the number one bank globally by market capitalization. 

                      It is the largest bank in the United States.

                      As of 2025, it is the world's largest bank by market capitalization. 

                      Headquarters: The global headquarters is located in New York City.


                      213

                      In Pakistan, financial futures including gold, silver, oil are traded in?

                      Correct Answer: Pakistan Mercantile Exchange
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      In Pakistan, financial futures for commodities like gold, silver, and oil are traded on the Pakistan Mercantile Exchange Limited (PMEX).

                      It is the country's first futures commodity market and is regulated by the Securities and Exchange Commission of Pakistan (SECP). 

                      214

                      In which city was the first women's bank established in 1989?

                      Correct Answer: Karachi
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      First Women Bank Limited commenced its business on 02 December 1989.

                      First Women Bank Limited (FWBL) is a commercial bank located in Karachi.

                       Prime Minister Benazir Bhutto set up the first bank in December 1988. 


                      ND8-1-2023

                      215

                      First Women Bank Limited commenced its business on _______?

                      Correct Answer: 02 Dec 1989
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      First Women Bank Limited commenced its business on 02 December 1989.

                      First Women Bank Limited (FWBL) is a commercial bank located in Karachi

                       Prime Minister Benazir Bhutto. set up the first bank in December 1988. 


                      216

                      Which of the following is the function of State Bank of Pakistan?

                      Correct Answer: Regulate Monetary Policy
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      State Bank of Pakistan Act 1956 requires the Bank to "regulate the monetary and credit system of Pakistan" 

                      State Bank Of Pakistan
                      Founded 1-Jul-1948
                      Founded byM.A Jinnah
                      First GovernorZahid Hussain
                      First Female GovernorShamshad Akhtar
                      SBP Responsibitly implement monetary policy
                      HQKarachi, Sindh


                      217

                      State Bank of Pakistan is also known as?

                      Correct Answer: Central Bank of Pakistan
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      The State Bank of Pakistan (SBP) is the central bank of Pakistan.

                      It is also known as the SBP. 


                      Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah inaugurated the SBP on July 1, 1948. 

                      Its headquarters are in Karachi, Pakistan's financial capital. 

                      The SBP's operations are based on the State Bank of Pakistan Act of 1956. 

                      218

                      State Bank of Pakistan is run by which of the following ? Board of managers, Board of governors, Board of directors, None of these

                      Correct Answer: Board of directors
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      The State Bank of Pakistan (SBP) is governed by a Board of Directors, which oversees the bank’s affairs under the SBP Act.

                      219

                      Who was UN General Assembly President in 1962-1963?

                      Correct Answer: Sir Zuffarullah Khan
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Sir Muhammad Zafarullah Khan of Pakistan was the President of the United Nations General Assembly from 1962 to 1963.

                      He was Pakistan's first foreign minister and led the case for Pakistan's separate nation in the Radcliffe Commission.

                      He also served as a judge on the International Court of Justice from 1954 to 1958, and as its vice president from 1958 to 1961.

                      In 1970, he became the first and only Pakistani to serve as the President of the International Court of Justice.

                      220

                      The Pothohar Plateau contains the mineral deposits?

                      Correct Answer: All of these
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      The Pothohar Plateau also known as Pothwar, is a plateau in north-eastern Pakistan

                      the total area of about 5000 miles2

                      located between the Indus River and the Jhelum River

                      300-600 meters is the height of the Pothohar Plateau
                      ******

                      The Potwar Plateau includes parts of the current four districts:

                      Jhelum

                      Chakwal

                      Rawalpindi

                      Attock

                      ****

                      The Pothohar plateau is rich in minerals like gypsum, limestone, coal, oil, and rock salt.

                      Potohar Plateau سطح مرتفع پوٹھوہار .consists of:

                      District Rawalpindi District
                      Islamabad Capital Territory
                      Chakwal District
                      Jhelum District
                      Khushab District,
                      Mirpur District
                      Islamabad
                      Mianwali District,
                      Sargodha District (Northern Belt)
                      ND13-5-2023

                      221

                      Pakistan most important and major crop is ___

                      Correct Answer: Cotton
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      The most important crops are wheat, sugarcane, cotton, and rice, which together account for more than 75% of the value of total crop output. 

                      Cotton is the most important cash crop in Pakistan and cotton products export account for 55 percent of all foreign exchange earnings of the country.

                      ------

                      Major Kharif crops are:

                      1.       Rice 

                      2.       Sugarcane

                      3.       Cotton

                      4.       Maze

                      5.       Bajra

                      6.       Jawar

                      When the period of Kharif crop starts and when ends?

                      Strat: April-June,      

                      End:  Sept - Oct

                       

                      When the period of Rabi crop starts and when ends?

                      These crops are planted in winter from October to December

                      harvested in summer from March to May

                       

                      Which are Pakistan major seasons for agriculture crop?

                      Kharif & Rabi

                      222

                      The most important Cash Crop of Pakistan after Wheat is:

                      Correct Answer: Rice
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      There are five major crops namely:

                      1. Cotton
                      2. wheat
                      3. rice
                      4. sugar cane
                      5. maize

                      Cotton is the most important cash crop in Pakistan in terms of area and value addition.

                      After Wheat, Rice is the second main staple food crop

                      Rice second major exportable commodity after cotton.


                      ***

                      nd12-2022

                      223

                      Which Muslim leader was declared Man of the Year by the Muslim 500?

                      Correct Answer: Imran Khan
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      According to The Muslim 500, the "Man of the Year" for the 2020 edition was then-Prime Minister of Pakistan, Imran Khan.

                      The 2024 recipient was Syed Muhammad Naquib al-Attas, a Malaysian Islamic philosopher and academic.

                      224

                      Which famous personality of Pakistan served as only Pakistani and also first Asian to preside over International court of Justice and also served as President of UN General Assembly?

                      Correct Answer: M. Zafrullah Khan
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Sir Muhammad Zafrulla Khan is the first and only Pakistani (and first Asian) to preside over the International Court of Justice 


                      He was elected President of the seventeenth session of the General Assembly, serving from 1962 to 1963.

                      He was a member of Pakistan's first cabinet and served as the nation's first Foreign Minister.

                      He also served as Pakistan's Permanent Representative to the United Nations. 

                      225

                      Lyallpur is the old name of _____?

                      Correct Answer: Faisalabad
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      Faisalabad, formerly known as Lyallpur, is the third-most-populous city in Pakistan.
                      Second-largest in the eastern province of Punjab.
                      *******
                      Pakistan City old name list
                      1-        Lyallpur was the old Name of Faisalabad
                      2-        Mahmood pur was the old Name of Lahore
                      3-        Kalanchi was the old Name of Karachi
                      4-        Raj Shahi was the old Name of Islamabad
                      5-        Persha pur was the old Name of Peshawar
                      6-        Khan Pur was the old Name of Gujranwala
                      7-        Cambell pur was the old Name of Attock
                      8-        Salwan Kot was the old Name of Sialkot
                      9-        Shal Kot was the old Name of Quetta
                      10-   Neroon Kot was the old Name of Hyderabad
                      11-   Khan Garh was the old Name of Jaccoabad
                      12-   Montgomery was the old Name of Sahiwal
                      13-   Pepri was the old Name of Bin Qasim
                      14-   Hindu Bagh was the old Name of Muslim Bagh
                      15-   Ajodhan pur was the old Name of Pakpatten
                      16-   Fort Sanemars was the old Name of Zhob
                      17-   owdayana was the old Name of Swat

                      226

                      Which of the following________ is the third most populated city in Pakistan?

                      Correct Answer: Faislabad
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Faislabad.is the third most populated city in Pakistan

                      ***

                      Faisalabad is located in the northeast of Punjab.

                      The city covers an area of approximately 1230 square kilometers, comprising largely of flat or rolling plains.

                      It has a population of around 5 million

                      ***

                      Karachi is the most populated city in Pakistan

                      Karachi is the capital city of Sindh.

                      Karachi is the largest city in Pakistan and the twelfth largest city in the world.

                      The largest district-wise area of Pakistan is Chaghi

                      **

                      Pakistan is the world's 3rd most populated country

                      Karachi is the most populated city in Sindh

                      227

                      What was the function of the first constituent assembly of Pakistan?

                      Correct Answer: To frame the constitution
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      The function of the first constituent assembly of Pakistan was To frame the constitution.

                      It also served as the interim federal legislature (parliament) of the new dominion.

                      228

                      Indian National Congress (INC) was established during which time?

                      Correct Answer: Aligarh Educational Movement
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      The Indian National Congress was founded at Gokuldas Tejpal Sanskrit College in Bombay On 28 December 1885.

                      Allan Octavian Hume Place assumed office as the General Secretary,

                      Womesh Chunder Bonnerjee of Calcutta was elected first President.

                      Founders: Allan Octavian Hume Place

                      229

                      When is Quaid-e-Azam Day celebrated?

                      Correct Answer: 25 December
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Quaid-e-Azam Day is celebrated on 25 December. 

                      Mohammad Ali Jinnah Born: December 25, 1876, Karachi

                      Mohammed Ali Jinnah died on September 11, 1948, in Karachi, Pakistan.

                      Mohammed Ali Jinnah was the founder and first governor-general (1947–48) of Pakistan


                      230

                      The ____ city was the Birthplace of the Mughal Emperor Akbar was born.

                      Correct Answer: Umarkot
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Jalal uddin Muhammad Akbar was a Mughal emperor.

                      He was born in Umarkot (Sindh) in 1542.

                      He became Emperor in 1556.

                      He was died in 1605.

                      231

                      Where Hazarganji Chiltan National Park situated?

                      Correct Answer: Balochistan
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Hazarganji Chiltan National Park is a national park in the Mastung District of western Balochistan Province of Pakistan. 

                      232

                      Which of the following merges in the Jhelum River?

                      Correct Answer: Kunhar
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      The Kunhar River merges into the Jhelum River. 

                      It originates from Lulusar Lake in the Kaghan Valley, joining it near Muzaffarabad.

                      233

                      What was the old name of Faisalabad?

                      Correct Answer: Lyallpur
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      Pakistan City old name list
                      1-        Lyallpur was the old Name of Faisalabad
                      2-        Mahmood pur was the old Name of Lahore
                      3-        Kalanchi was the old Name of Karachi
                      4-        Raj Shahi was the old Name of Islamabad
                      5-        Persha pur was the old Name of Peshawar
                      6-        Khan Pur was the old Name of Gujranwala

                      8-        Salwan Kot was the old Name of Sialkot
