                      345

                      Who became first South Asian to make biggest throw and win Javelin Gold in CWG 2022?

                      Correct Answer: Arshad Nadeem
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      • The first javelin gold for Pakistan was won by Arshad Nadeem in Common Wealth Games in Birmingham, England in August 2022.

                      ***

                      • Arshad Nadeem won a gold medal for his record-breaking 90.18-meter throw.
                      • Arshad Nadeem became the first South Asian and second Asian to win this title.

                      ***

                      •  Arshad Nadeem is a Pakistani javelin thrower.
                      • He represents WAPDA in the domestic competition.
                      • He is the first Pakistani to qualify for the final at the Olympic Games and the World Athletics Championships. 
                      • Born: January 2, 1997 (age 25 years), Khanewal

                      ***

                      346

                      Which of the following was most stricken by the floods in 2022? Balochistan, Khyber-Pakhtunkhwa, Sindh, None of these

                      Correct Answer: Sindh
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Economic Impact: Sindh suffered 70% of Pakistan’s total flood-related losses and damages.

                      Population Affected: Over 14.5 million people affected in 23 districts; 33 million affected nationwide.

                      Infrastructure Damage: 2 million+ houses, 20,000 schools, 1,000 health facilities damaged; 64% of total road damage occurred in Sindh.

                      Agricultural Damage: 4.8 million acres of crops inundated, affecting cotton, rice, and sugarcane.

                      Public Health: Nearly half of deaths and 66% of injuries in Sindh; rise in waterborne diseases like malaria.

                      Water Inundation: By mid-August 2022, one-third of Sindh was under floodwater, lasting several months in some areas.

                      347

                      Which of the following is the newest district of Sindh? Keamari, Badin, Matiari, None of these

                      Correct Answer: Keamari
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Keamari District is the newest district in Sindh province, Pakistan.

                      It was officially established in August 2020.

                      The district was created by bifurcating Karachi West District.

                      Keamari became the seventh district of Karachi.

                      Sindh Cabinet approved the creation of Keamari District on August 20, 2020.

                      Chief Minister Murad Ali Shah’s spokesperson announced the approval.

                      Keamari is the 30th district of Sindh.

                      The district includes Site, Baldia, Harbour, and Maripur sub-divisions.

                      Karachi West District, from which Keamari was carved, is the largest district in Sindh by population.

                      Karachi West District had a population of 3,914,757.

                      348

                      What is the capital of Gilgit Baltistan?

                      Correct Answer: Gilgit city
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                        Capital of Gilgit-Baltistan is Gilgit city

                        Gilgit Baltistan is located on the North side of Pakistan

                        Gilgit is a city of: Gilgit-Baltistan Pakistan

                        Gilgit-Baltistan, formerly known as the Northern Areas, is a region administered by Pakistan as an administrative territory.

                        Gilgit-Baltistan was Established on 1 November 1948

                        Largest city of Gilgit-Baltistan is Skardu

                        349

                        Which of the following was given Nishan-e-Haider? Gen. Pervaiz Musharaf, Gen. Raheel Sharif, Maj. Shabbir Sharif, None of these

                        Correct Answer: Maj. Shabbir Sharif
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        Nishan-e-Haider literally means Emblem of Lion.

                        1. Muhammad Sarwar Punjab Regiment, Pakistan Army Captain Indo-Pakistani War of 1947 27 July 1948

                        2. Saif Ali Janjua 5 Azad Kashmir regiment (HAIDER DIL)/18 Azad Kashmir Regiment, Pakistan Army Naik Indo-Pakistani War of 1947 26 October 1948

                        3. Tufail Mohammad 13 Punjab Regiment, Border Guards East Pakistan Rifles, Pakistan Army Major Indo-Pakistani border skirmishes 1958 7 August 1958

                        4. Raja Aziz Bhatti 17 Punjab Regiment, Pakistan Army Major Indo-Pakistani War of 1965 10 September 1965

                        5.  Rashid Minhas No. 2 Fighter Conversion Unit, Pakistan Air Force Pilot Officer Indo-Pakistani War of 1971 20 August 1971

                        6. Muhammad Akram 4 Frontier Force Regiment, Pakistan Army Major Indo-Pakistani War of 1971 5 December 1971

                        7. Shabbir Sharif 6 Frontier Force Regiment, Pakistan Army Major Indo-Pakistani War of 1971 6 December 1971

                        8. Muhammad Hussain 20 Lancers (Armoured Corps), Pakistan Army Sowar Indo-Pakistani War of 1971 10 December 1971

                        9. Muhammad Mahfuz Janjua 15 Punjab Regiment, Pakistan Army Lance naik Indo-Pakistani War of 1971 17 December 1971

                        10. Karnal Sher Khan 12 Northern Light Infantry, Pakistan Army Captain Kargil War 5 July 1999

                        11. Lalak Jan 12 Northern Light Infantry, Pakistan Army Havildar Kargil War 7 July 1999

                        350

                        Which is the biggest textile market in Pakistan?

                        Correct Answer: Faisalabad
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        Faisalabad is the largest textile market in Pakistan.

                        It is known as the "Manchester of Pakistan" due to its significant textile industry.

                        Gadoon Textile Mills Limited, established in 1988.

                        It is one of the largest spinning units in the country.

                        351

                        Pakistan's aim for Electric Vehicle (EV) sales by 2030 is _____?

                        Correct Answer: 30%
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        Pakistan's National Electric Vehicle (NEV) Policy 2025 aims for 30% of all new vehicle sales to be electric by 2030.

                        This policy is a strategic plan to promote green transportation, reduce reliance on imported fuels, lower air pollution, and foster economic growth through new jobs and the development of an EV industry.

                        The government is introducing incentives and fast-tracking processes to support this transition. 

                        352

                        The naval exercise between China and Pakistan is known as _____?

                        Correct Answer: Sea Guardian
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        The joint naval exercise between China and Pakistan is primarily known as Sea Guardian.

                        The air force exercise is called Shaheen (Eagle). 

                        353

                        In 2019, the state of Jammu & Kashmir was reorganized and bifurcated into which of the following?

                        Correct Answer: Two separate Union Territories
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        Reorganization (2019):

                        The state of Jammu & Kashmir was reorganized and bifurcated into two Union Territories.

                        New Union Territories:

                        Union Territory of Jammu & Kashmir (with a legislative assembly)

                        Union Territory of Ladakh (without a legislature)

                        Legal Basis:

                        Enacted by the Jammu and Kashmir Reorganisation Act, 2019, passed by the Indian Parliament.

                        Effective Date:

                        Came into effect on October 31, 2019.

                        Constitutional Change:

                        Included the revocation of Article 370 of the Indian Constitution, which had granted special autonomous status to the region.

                        354

                        The armed conflict between Pakistan and India in 2025 lasted for _____?

                        Correct Answer: 4
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        Duration: The armed conflict between Pakistan and India lasted four days, from May 7 to May 10, 2025.

                        Conclusion: Hostilities ended after a ceasefire agreement was reached.

                        Means of Agreement: Ceasefire was negotiated via hotline communication between the Directors General of Military Operations (DGMOs) of both countries.

                        Diplomatic Involvement: The United States contributed to diplomatic efforts to de-escalate the situation.

                        355

                        Nizamuddin Auliya was associated with which Sufi order?

                        Correct Answer: Chishti
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation
                        Muhammad Nizamuddin Auliya:
                        • Also known as Hazrat Nizamuddin and Mahbub-e-Ilahi.
                        • Indian Sunni Muslim scholar and Sufi saint of the Chishti Order.
                        • One of the most famous Sufis of the Indian Subcontinent.
                        Baba Fariduddin Ganj-e-Shakar (Baba Farid):
                        • Disciple of Qutbuddin Bhaktiyar Kaki (namesake of Qutab Minar).
                        • Settled in Ajodhan, Punjab, and built a Jamaat Khana.
                        Three disciples founded sub-orders:
                        1. Nizamiyya order – founded by Nizamuddin Auliya.
                        2. Jamaliyya order – founded by Jamaluddin Hanovi.
                        3. Sabaria order – founded by Alauddin Sabin.
                        356

                        What is the name of the proposed Russia-Pakistan gas pipeline project?

                        Correct Answer: Pakistan Stream Gas Pipeline
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        The Russia-Pakistan gas pipeline project is named the Pakistan Stream Gas Pipeline (PSGP).

                        It was formerly known as the North-South gas pipeline, and sometimes as PakSteam.

                        The project is designed to transport liquefied natural gas from Karachi to Kasur.

                        357

                        What is the primary reason behind the Iran-Pakistan gas pipeline project?

                        Correct Answer: To export Iranian natural gas to meet Pakistan's energy needs
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        The pipeline is intended to supply Iranian natural gas to Pakistan to overcome its severe electricity and gas shortages.

                        It strengthens bilateral economic relations by providing Pakistan with a sustainable energy source and Iran with a stable export market.

                        358

                        Mir Chakar Khan Rind belonged to which ethnic group?

                        Correct Answer: Baloch
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        Name: Chakar Khan Rind (1468–1565)

                        Native Name: میر چاکَر خان رِند (Balochi)

                        Role: Baloch chieftain

                        Achievement: Founded the Second Baloch Confederacy (1487–1512)

                        Legacy:

                        • Considered a folk hero of the Baloch people
                        • Important figure in the Baloch epic Hani and Sheh Mureed
                        359

                        Which countries established Regional Cooperation for Development (RCD) in the year 1964?

                        Correct Answer: Iran, Pakistan and Turkey
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        RCD was established on 21 July 1964

                        It was dissolved in 1979

                        RCD stands for Regional Cooperation for Development

                        They established by Iran, Pakistan,and Turkey

                        Its headquarters were located in Iran

                        360

                        What is the Height of Tirich Mir peak?

                        Correct Answer: 7708 meters
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        Tirich Mir is the highest mountain in the Hindu Kush range

                        It’s the 33rd-highest peak in the world.

                        It is the highest mountain in the world

                        Tirich Mir is located in the Chitral District of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa

                        Tirich Mir height: 7708 meters 

                        Its first recorded climber on 21 July 1950 by a Norwegian


                        Additional Information:

                        Pakistan Highest Peaks List:

                        Himalayas range: Nanga Parbat

                        K-2: Krakarm range

                        Kirthar: Zardak peak

                        Koh e Suleman: Takhat e Sulaimani

                        Salt range: Sakesar

                        Minimum age limit to contest for MNA in Pakistan is _____?

                        Correct Answer: 25 years

                        362

                        Astola and Manora islands are located in which sea?

                        Correct Answer: Arabian Sea
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        Astola and Manora are coastal islands of Pakistan, both located in the Arabian Sea.

                        Astola is Pakistan’s largest offshore island, while Manora is near Karachi.

                        King of Swing: Wasim Akram

                        Black Superman: Muhammad Ali
                        365

                        What was the capital of Pakistan immediately after partition in 1947?

                        Correct Answer: Karachi
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        After the partition of British India in 1947, the capital of Pakistan was Karachi.

                        In 1963, Islamabad was established as the new capital of Pakistan, replacing Karachi.

                        Reasons for Shifting Capital: Islamabad's location in the northern part of the country provided better accessibility to all regions of Pakistan, unlike Karachi

                        366

                        Who won Pakistan's first medal in Islamic Solidarity Games 2025?

                        Correct Answer: Fatima Zahra
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        Boxer Fatima Zahra confirmed the first medal for Pakistan at the 6th Islamic Solidarity Games taking place in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia in November 2025.

                        She qualified for the -60 kg semifinals on 6 Nov 2025 night as she defeated her Algerian opponent, Hamda Melissa, 5-0 in the quarterfinal bout.

                        She confirmed a bronze medal.

                        She belongs to Sargodha and has been boxing since 2018.

                        She is a Pakistan Army athlete.

                        ***

                        367

                        Name the Pakistani shooters who won silver and bronze medal in Asian Shooting Grand Prix 2025 held in Kuwait?

                        Correct Answer: Usman Chand & Imam Haroon
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        Pakistan’s shooters won silver and bronze medals in the rifle skeet competition at the Asian Shotgun Grand Prix Championship 2025 in Kuwait.

                        Usman Chand clinched the silver medal, while Imam Haroon secured bronze.

                        ****

                        368

                        Name the constable of Lahore police who won Gold Medal in World Fitness Federation Championship 2025?

                        Correct Answer: Asim Raza
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        Lahore Police constable Asim Raza has brought pride to Pakistan by winning a gold medal and a Pro Card at the World Fitness Federation (WFF) Universe 2025 Championship held in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia.

                        Asim Raza excelled in the Amateur Men’s Classic Bodybuilding Performance category, defeating contestants from South Africa, Russia and India to claim the gold medal. 


                        27 countries participated in this competition. 

                        369

                        Which City of Pakistan was destroyed in the Earthquake of 1935?

                        Correct Answer: Quetta
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation
                        • On 31 May 1936 Quetta was devasted by an earthquake;
                        • ****
                        • An earthquake occurred on 31 May 1935
                        • An earthquake occurred between 2:33 am and 3:40 am at Quetta.
                        • The causalities were up to 60,000
                        • ***

                        Related Question to Earthquake:

                        • Attabad lake emerged in the Hunza valley due to an Earthquake
                        • A powerful earthquake killed at least 1000 people and left scores injured in Afghanistan in the early hours on 22 June 2022.
                        • Who came to Pakistan after the earthquake as a special representative of UNO's earthquake relief? George Bush Sr.
                        • Alaska U.S. state has had the most earthquakes
                        • Which two countries witnessed a massive earthquake on October 30, 2020? Turkey and Greece
                        • Which province was mostly hit by an earthquake in Afghanistan in June 2022? Paktika
                        •  ***
                        • An earthquake is a Natural Disaster
                        • An activity related to an earthquake is Seismic activity
                        • An earthquake can be simplified as a Shaking of the earth
                          The intensity of an earthquake is measured by: the Richter scale
                        • The earthquake had a magnitude of 7.7 Mw
                        • ****
                        • Richter Scale was developed in 1935.
                        • Seismology is the study of earthquakes.
                        370

                        Pakistan conducted nuclear tests on May 28, 1998 at?

                        Correct Answer: Chaghi Hills
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        Pakistan conducted a series of nuclear tests on 28 May 1998

                        conducted at the Chagai test site, in Balochistan.

                        5 Nuclear tests were conducted at 15:15 hrs PST on 28 May 1998.

                        The tests were performed at Ras Koh Hills in the Chagai District 

                        it was Pakistan's first public test of nuclear weapons.

                        while 

                        Chagai II

                        Chagai-II is the codename assigned to the second atomic test conducted by Pakistan, carried out on 30 May 1998

                        These tests followed the Chagai-I series

                        The test site was Kharan Desert, Pakistan

                        The number of tests is 1

                        The Sindh Museum was establish in 1971 to collect, preserve, study, and exhibit the records of the cultural history of Sindh and Indus Valley Civilization.

                        The SAARC movement was launched for Regional cooperation

                        Headquarters: Kathmandu

                        373

                        Which of the following does not border with Sindh? Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Balochistan, Arabian Sea, Punjab

                        Correct Answer: Khyber Pakhtunkhwa
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        Borders of Sindh:

                        Sindh is bordered by:

                        • Balochistan to the west and north-west
                        • Punjab to the north
                        • Arabian Sea to the south
                        • Indian states (Rajasthan and Gujarat) to the east

                        No direct border:

                        Khyber Pakhtunkhwa does not share a direct border with Sindh; Punjab lies between them.

                        What is the total area of Balochistan Province?

                        Correct Answer: 347,190 Sq Km
                        Show explanation
                        Explanation

                        "Baluchistan" important question and information:

                        Baluchistan covers 42 percent of the area of Pakistan

                        Baluchistan province is located in South West of Pakistan.

                          Baluchistan became a province in 1970.

                            Capital of Baluchistan is Quetta.

                              Baluchistan is the biggest province of Pakistan by area.

                              Balochistan is located on the Iranian plateau.

                              Sajji dish belongs to Balochistan

                              35 districts are there in Balochistan

                              There are many rivers in Balochistan but 7 are the main

                              347,190 sq Km is the total area of the Balochistan Province

                              Hingol is the largest river in the Balochistan

                              Baluchistan province has 7 divisions.

                                Kalat

                                  Makran

                                    Nasirabad

                                      Quetta

                                        Sibi

                                            379

                                            The 1973 Constitution of Pakistan came into force on?

                                            Correct Answer: 14th August 1973
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation
                                               
                                            • The 1973 Constitution of Pakistan was Passed on April 10, 1973
                                            • Approved on April 12
                                            • Enforced on August 14, 1973
                                            • Drafted by Zulfikar Ali Bhutto's government,
                                            • This Constitution is called the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan 1973
                                            Additional information
                                            Feature of the 1973 Constitution
                                            (Majlis-e-Shoora) consisting of the National Assembly and the Senate.
                                            Declares Islam as the state religion
                                            Requires all laws to conform to the Quran and Sunnah.

                                            Last updated on 09-03-2026


                                            380

                                            Indus water treaty was signed between _____?

                                            Correct Answer: India and Pakistan
                                            Show explanation
                                            Explanation
                                            • The Indus Waters Treaty, signed on September 19, 1960, between India and Pakistan and brokered by the World Bank.
                                              • It allocates the Western Rivers (Indus, Jhelum, Chenab) to Pakistan and the Eastern Rivers (Ravi, Beas, Sutlej) to India.
                                              • It was signed in Karachi by Pakistan President Ayub Khan, Indian Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru, and Mr. W.A.B. Illif. 
                                              • The Treaty became effective on April 1, 1960.
                                              381

                                              In 1916, where was the joint session of the All India Muslim League and the Indian National Congress held?

                                              Correct Answer: Lucknow
                                              Show explanation
                                              Explanation
                                              • The Lucknow Pact was an agreement between the Indian National Congress (INC) and the All-India Muslim League in December 1916.
                                                • Led by Bal Gangadhar Tilak for the INC and Muhammad Ali Jinnah for the Muslim League.
                                                • The Lucknow Pact allowed representation for religious minorities in provincial legislatures.
                                                • It was adopted by the Congress on December 29 and by the League on December 31, 1916.
                                                382

                                                The government of Benazir Bhutto was dismissed for the second time on November 1996 by _____?

                                                Correct Answer: President of Pakistan Farooq Leghari
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation

                                                Farooq Ahmad Khan Leghari was the eighth president of Pakistan

                                                eighth president of Pakistan from 14 November 1993 until resigning on 2 December 1997.

                                                He was the first Baloch to be elected as president.

                                                He started his own Millat party 

                                                In 1996 he dismissed Benazir Government

                                                Born: Sardar Farooq Ahmad Khan Leghari 29 May 1940

                                                Died: 20 October 2010 (aged 70) Rawalpindi, Pakistan

                                                Resting place: Choti Zareen, Dera Ghazi Khan, Pakistan

                                                Political party: Pakistan Muslim League (Q) (2002–2010)

                                                383

                                                Which country uses GMT+5 as its standard time?

                                                Correct Answer: Pakistan
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation
                                                • Pakistan's standard time is 5 hours from Greenwich means Time
                                                • ***
                                                • Pakistan's standard time was suggested by Professor Muhammad Anwar in 1951
                                                • **
                                                • Greenwich Mean Time (GMT) is the standard time of which of UK
                                                384

                                                +92 is the country code of _____?

                                                Correct Answer: Pakistan
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation
                                                The County codes were introduced in 1960 by International Telecon Union.
                                                PK is the national code of Pakistan.
                                                +92 is the dialing code of Pakistan. 
                                                385

                                                Which of the following languages has the most alphabets? English, Urdu, Sindhi, None of these

                                                Correct Answer: Sindhi
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation

                                                The Sindhi language, which is based on a modified Arabic script, has 52 alphabetic characters. 

                                                The English alphabet has 26 letters.

                                                The Urdu alphabet has 39 letters.

                                                386

                                                Who was the first democratically elected female prime minister of Pakistan?

                                                Correct Answer: Benazir Bhutto
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation

                                                Benazir Bhutto became the first female prime minister of Pakistan in 1988

                                                As Prime Minister first time from 1988 to 1990 

                                                 As Prime Minister second time from 1993 to 1996.

                                                She was the first woman to head a democratic government in a Muslim-majority nation.

                                                Benazir Bhutto was a Pakistani politician who served as the 11th and 13th prime ministers of Pakistan 

                                                Bhutto Pakistan's second nationally elected Prime Minister.

                                                Benazir Bhutto was born on June 21, 1953

                                                Benazir Bhutto died On 8 February 2008

                                                Benazir Bhutto married Asif Ali Zardari on December 18, 1987 

                                                Awards: United Nations Prize in the Field of Human Rights

                                                ND28-4-2023



                                                387

                                                Who was the first person to use the term Two Nation Theory in 1867?

                                                Correct Answer: Sir Syed Ahmed Khan
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation

                                                Sir Syed is considered as the first person to theorize the idea of separate nationhood for Muslims in subcontinent.

                                                In a speech at Meerut in 1866 he presented on overall scenario of post colonial phase in

                                                which he described Muslims and Hindus as two nations

                                                Syed Ahmad Khan who presented the theory of the two nations 

                                                He presented this theory In a speech at Meerut in 1866 

                                                The two-nation theory is the basis of the creation of Pakistan.


                                                The theory advocated by the All India Muslim League is the founding principle of the Pakistan Movement.

                                                The state of India officially rejected the two-nation theory


                                                Aga Khan

                                                He called on the British Raj to consider Muslims to be a separate nation from India,

                                                the so-called 'Two Nation Theory"

                                                Abdul

                                                Ghaffar

                                                khan

                                                He founded the Khudai Khidmatgar commonly known as the RED SHIRTS,

                                                during the 1920s

                                                Awarded Bharat Ratna in 1987

                                                Abbas 

                                                Tyabji

                                                He is also known as the Grand Old Man of Gujarat

                                                Served as the Chief Justice of Baroda State

                                                Tyabji launched the Dharasana Satyagraha. in 1930



                                                agm19.3.23


                                                388

                                                The Lucknow Pact was adopted by the Congress in

                                                Correct Answer: December 1916
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation

                                                On 1st October 1906, a Muslim political delegation led by Aga Khan

                                                Met with the Viceroy of India to demand a separate electorate for Muslims.

                                                This delegation played a crucial role in the establishment of the Muslim League in 1906

                                                389

                                                Quaid-e-Azam presented famous ________ in reply of Nehru report.

                                                Correct Answer: 14 points
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation

                                                The Nehru Report was published on August 28, 1928, at the Lucknow conference of all the parties.

                                                the draft constitution was prepared which was called the “Nehru Committee Report“.

                                                Jinnah Voted against this report.

                                                And represented 14 points in the reply to Nehru Report

                                                List of Jinnah's fourteen points (presents in 1929 in response of )

                                                Title                               Description
                                                Point 1:Federal System
                                                Point 2:Provincial Autonomy
                                                Point 3:Representation of Minorities
                                                Point 4:Number of Muslim Representative
                                                Point 5:Separate Electorates
                                                Point 6:Muslim Majority Provinces
                                                Point 7:Religious Liberty
                                                Point 8:Three-Fourth Representation
                                                Point 9:Separation of Sindh
                                                Point 10:Introduction of Reforms in N.W.F.P and Baluchistan
                                                Point 11:Government Services
                                                Point 12:Protection of Muslim’s culture and Language
                                                Point 13:One-Third Muslim Ministers
                                                Point 14:

                                                Constitution

                                                390

                                                Who opposed the separate electorate?

                                                Correct Answer: Congress and other Hindu bodies
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation

                                                All important Question Related to " Separate Electorate" 

                                                Indian Councils Act of 1909, also called Morley-Minto Reforms

                                                Minto-Morley Reforms Muslim's demand for a separate electorate was incorporated

                                                In 12 March 1909 the Morley-Minto Changes allowed partitioned electorates to Muslims.

                                                This Act further extended democratic participation by increasing the size of the legislative councils, both Central and provincial.

                                                27 out of add up to 60 members were to be chosen and 5 seats were saved for the Muslims.

                                                Indian Council Act 1909 separate electorate for Muslims was accepted

                                                on 28 August 1928 Nehru Report rejected the demand for a separate electorate

                                                Congress and other Hindu bodies opposed the separate electorate

                                                ND30-4-2023

                                                391

                                                In which year M.A.O College became University?

                                                Correct Answer: 1920
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation
                                                • The Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental College was founded by Sir Syed Ahmad Khan in 1875, initially as a primary school, with the intention of turning it into a college-level institution.
                                                • In 1877, the school was raised to college level.
                                                • Lord Lytton, the Viceroy of India at the time, laid the foundation stone of the Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental College (MAO College) on January 8, 1878. The college was later upgraded to Aligarh Muslim University (AMU) in 1920.

                                                He served as India’s first Education Minister post-1947.

                                                398

                                                Tarbela dam is situated in?

                                                Correct Answer: KPK
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation

                                                Tarbela Dam is an earth-filled dam along the Indus River in swabi  Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province.

                                                Tarbela Dam is the largest earth-filled dam in the world, and also the largest dam by structural volume

                                                Tarbela Dam Construction began in 1968  and  Opening date 1976
                                                399

                                                The Article 256 is?

                                                Correct Answer: Private armies
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation

                                                Article 256 of the Constitution of Pakistan forbids the formation of any private organization

                                                That can function as a military organization.

                                                This means such organizations are illegal and any that are formed will be considered unlawful.

                                                This article is a part of the country's constitution, which was adopted on April 10, 1973. 


                                                400

                                                Write the following word stands for:NUTECH

                                                Correct Answer: National University of Technology
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation

                                                NUTECH : National University of Technology

                                                It is a public university located in Islamabad, Pakistan.

                                                It was established in 2017

                                                It as an affiliate university of the Frontier Works Organization (FWO), a department of Pakistan Army.

                                                Motto

                                                رہبرِ ترقی و کمال

                                                Leading Towards Progress and Excellence

                                                401

                                                KRL Stands for ________?

                                                Correct Answer: Khan Research Laboratories
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation

                                                KRL Stands for Khan Research Laboratories

                                                The Dr. A. Q. Khan Research Laboratories, also known as KRL.

                                                The laboratory is located in Kahuta, Rawalpindi, Punjab.

                                                KRL Established .31 July 1976
                                                402

                                                PS stands for _______?

                                                Correct Answer: Province of Sindh
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation

                                                Leader of the Opposition of Sindh Assembly

                                                Haleem Adil Sheikh PTI since 26 January 2021

                                                403

                                                OGRA stands for _____?

                                                Correct Answer: Oil and Gas Regulatory Authority
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation

                                                OGRA stands for Oil and Gas Regulatory Authority.

                                                It is the regulatory body responsible for overseeing the oil and gas sector in Pakistan.

                                                It was established in 2002.

                                                It to ensure a fair and transparent regulatory framework for the exploration, production, transportation, and distribution of oil and gas resources.

                                                404

                                                PPI stands for ______?

                                                Correct Answer: Pakistan Press International
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation
                                              • Pakistan Press International (PPI) was established in June 1956.
                                              • It was initially founded as the Pakistan Press Association (PPA).
                                                • 405

                                                IBCC stands for _____?

                                                Correct Answer: Inter Board Coordination Commission
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation
                                              • IBCC (Inter Board Coordination Commission) is a federal education body in Pakistan established in 1972.
                                              • Key responsibilities include standardizing exams, recognizing local and foreign qualifications, and promoting curricular and extracurricular activities.
                                              • Advises and facilitates teacher and student exchanges and represents the Higher Education Commission (HEC).
                                                • 406

                                                "K" in Pakistan stands for ______?

                                                Correct Answer: Kashmir
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation

                                                The word was first introduced now or never in 1933 by Choudhry Rahmat Ali.

                                                The "Pakistan Declaration" titled Now or Never means Are We to Live or Perish Forever?

                                                In this pamphlet, the word Pakistan was used for the first time 

                                                This pamphlet was derived from the two-nation theory of Muslim reformer Syed Ahmed Khan

                                                it was a pamphlet, referring to the names of the five northern regions of British India: 

                                                1. Punjab
                                                2. Afghanistan
                                                3. Kashmir
                                                4. Sindh
                                                5. Baluchistan. (Pakistan)

                                                ***

                                                407

                                                IPCC stands for _______?

                                                Correct Answer: Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation
                                                IPCC stands for Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change.
                                                IPCC endowment under United Nation.
                                                It studies scientific reason of Climate change and it's impact on economic and political life of people.
                                                408

                                                ESPN is one of the biggest satellite TV networks. It stands for ______?

                                                Correct Answer: Entertainment and Sports Programming Network
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation
                                              • ESPN stands for Entertainment and Sports Programming Network, a major sports broadcasting channel.

                                              • It was founded in 1979.
                                              • It is known for covering various international sports events.
                                                • 409

                                                PNA stands for what?

                                                Correct Answer: Pakistan National Alliance
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation
                                              • Pakistan National Alliance (PNA) was a political alliance of nine opposition parties formed in 1977.

                                              • It opposed Zulfikar Ali Bhutto's government and protested against alleged election rigging.
                                                • 410

                                                SNGPL stands for _____?

                                                Correct Answer: Sui Northern Gas Pipelines Limited
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation

                                                SNGPL stand for Sui Northern Gas Pipelines Limited.

                                                SNGPL  was incorporated as a private limited Company in 1963.

                                                Converted into a public limited company in January 1964.

                                                ***

                                                RY5022023
                                                411

                                                WAF stands for ______?

                                                Correct Answer: Women's Action Forum
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation

                                                About WAF

                                                Full form: Women's Action Forum

                                                Founding place: Karachi

                                                Founding year: 1981

                                                Type: Women's organization

                                                Purpose: To protect women's rights

                                                412

                                                NAB stands for ______?

                                                Correct Answer: National Accountability Bureau
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation

                                                The National Accountability Bureau (NAB) is Pakistan's lead anti-corruption agency.

                                                It established by Pervez Musharraf on November 16, 1999.

                                                Its headquarters are in Islamabad.

                                                It focuses on combating political corruption and ensuring accountability.

                                                413

                                                What is the old name of Bahawalnagar district?

                                                Correct Answer: روجھان والی
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation

                                                ماضی میں ریاست بہاولپور کا ایک ضلع تھا۔

                                                نواب سر صادق محمد خاں عباسی خامس پنجم کے مورثِ اعلیٰ کے نام پر بہاول نگر نام رکھا گیا۔

                                                414

                                                ننکانہ صاحب کس شہر کے قریب واقع ہے

                                                Correct Answer: لاہور
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation

                                                ننکانہ صاحب پاکستان کے صوبہ پنجاب کا ایک شہر ہے جو ضلع ننکانہ صاحب کا ضلعی ہیڈ کوآرٹر بھی ہے۔ ضلع ننکانہ صاحب میں کل چار تحصیلیں شامل ہیں جو ننکانہ صاحب، صفدرآباد، سانگلہ ہل اور شاہ کوٹ ہیں۔

                                                شہر راۓپور (Raipur) اور رائے بھوئی دی تلونڈی (Rai-Bhoi-di-Talwandi) کے نام سے بھی جانا جاتا ہے۔ شہر کی آبادی لگ بھگ ساٹھ ہزار افراد پر مشتمل ہے اور شہر کا فاصلہ صوبائی دار الحکومت لاہور سے 75 کلومیٹر مغرب میں

                                                ہے۔ شہر کو سکھ مذہب کا سب سے مقدس مقام قرار دیا جاتا ہے اور سکھ مذہب کے بانی بابا گرو نانک دیو کی جائے پیدائش ہے۔


                                                415

                                                Mirpur Khas is famous for Cultivation of___________?

                                                Correct Answer: Mangoes
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation

                                                Mirpur Khas is the capital city of Mirpur Khas Division in the Sindh province.

                                                It is the 5th largest city in Sindh province and the 33rd largest city of Pakistan.

                                                The city was built by Talpur rulers of Mankani branch. 

                                                District: Mirpur Khas

                                                Province: Sindh

                                                416

                                                Mirpur is a famous city of?

                                                Correct Answer: Azad Kashmir
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation

                                                which has two subdivisions Mirpur and Dadyal.
                                                Mirpur is now one of the                                                 largest city of Azad Kashmir.

                                                RK4-12-22
                                                417

                                                Hakla-D.I. Khan Motorway, inaugurated on 5 January 2022 is also known as _____?

                                                Correct Answer: M14
                                                Show explanation
                                                Explanation
                                                • The M-14 Motorway, also known as the Islamabad–Dera Ismail Khan Motorway or Hakla–Yarik Motorway.
                                                  • It is a four-lane north-south motorway in Pakistan.
                                                  • It spans 285 km (177 mi) and has been operational since January 5, 2022.
                                                  • The motorway features 11 interchanges, 36 bridges, 33 flyovers, and 119 underpasses.
                                                  418

                                                  Kafir Fort is located in the Province _______?

                                                  Correct Answer: Khyber Pakhtunkhwa
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation

                                                  Kafir Kot, also known as Kafirkot.

                                                  It is located in the Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province of Pakistan.

                                                  It's in the Dera Ismail Khan district, near the cities of Kundian and Mianwali in Punjab. 

                                                  419

                                                  Old name of Kot Diji?

                                                  Correct Answer: Fort Ahmadabad
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation

                                                  Kot Diji  was the forerunner of the Indus Civilization. 

                                                  Kot Diji Fort was built around 1790 by the Talpur dynasty ruler of Upper Sindh, Mir Suhrab, who reigned from 1783 to 1830 AD.

                                                  This fort built on the ridge of a steep narrow hill is well-preserved.

                                                  420

                                                  The Southern border area of Khairpur district in Sindh is known as:

                                                  Correct Answer: Nara desert
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation

                                                  Nara Desert Wildlife Sanctuary is located in Mirpurkhas District, Sindh, Pakistan.

                                                  The Nara desert provides refuge to much wildlife in the Sindh province.

                                                  421

                                                  Which Dam was the first build in Pakistan?

                                                  Correct Answer: Warsak
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation
                                                   Warsak Dam is the oldest dam of Pakistan.
                                                  Warsak dam is situated on river Kabul.
                                                  Warsak Dam Construction began in 1949.
                                                  Warsak Dam Opening date 1960.

                                                  =====
                                                  Others dams info

                                                  The other was the Tarbela dam on the river Indus.
                                                  Mangla was the first of 2 dams.
                                                  The Mangla Dam is situated on the Jhelum River in the Mirpur District of AJK,
                                                  It is the seventh-largest dam in the world.
                                                  Construction began in 1961 Opening date 1965
                                                  The Mangla Dam components include 5 tunnels and a power station.

                                                  422

                                                  Partition of Bangal was made by?

                                                  Correct Answer: Lord Curzon
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation
                                                  Partition of Bengal was made by Lord Curzon
                                                  ***
                                                  Lord Curzon issued the order to partition Bengal on 16 October 1915.
                                                  Lord Curzon divided Bengal into 2 provinces
                                                  Bengal was divided into two provinces: in 1905.
                                                  Partition of Bengal came into force on 16 October 1905
                                                  Hindus and Congress agitated for the Partition of Bengal.
                                                  ***
                                                  Warren Hastings was governor-general of Bengal from 1772 to 1785.

                                                  ND9-11-2022


                                                  423

                                                  Which is the oldest city of Pakistan ?

                                                  Correct Answer: Peshawar
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation

                                                  The most ancient city of South Asia is Peshawar

                                                  Peshawar is the capital of the Pakistani province of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa.

                                                  It is the sixth-largest city in Pakistan, 

                                                  largest Pashtun-majority city.

                                                  Peshawar's recorded history dates back to at least 539 BCE, making it the oldest city in Pakistan and one of the oldest cities in South Asia.

                                                  424

                                                  Cherry is mostly found in which city of Pakistan?

                                                  Correct Answer: Gilgit-Baltistan
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation

                                                  Cherries are mostly found in the Gilgit-Baltistan region of Pakistan,

                                                  It is the country's main hub for cherry production, contributing around 70% of the total national output. 

                                                  The other major cherry-growing region in Pakistan is the province of Balochistan, 
                                                  Including areas like Quetta and Ziarat. 

                                                  425

                                                  Poonch river is the tributory of which river???

                                                  Correct Answer: Jhelum
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation

                                                  The Poonch River is a tributary of the Jhelum River.

                                                  It flows through the regions of Jammu and Kashmir (India) and Azad Jammu and Kashmir (Pakistan) before it joins the Jhelum River.  

                                                  Frederic Drew wrote of the Poonch River in 1875

                                                  426

                                                  Everest is the world's highest mountain, what is the name of these second highest?

                                                  Correct Answer: K2
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation

                                                  K-2 is Also Known As Godwin Austin

                                                  'K' standing for Karakoram.

                                                  Mount K2 is the second-highest mountain on Earth.

                                                   after the world's highest peak Mount Everest.

                                                  Mount K2 is part of the Karakorum Range located on the border of Pakistan and China.

                                                  K2 height 8,611 meters

                                                  ******

                                                  ND1-1-2023


                                                  427

                                                  The Chauri Chaura Incident occurred in which year?

                                                  Correct Answer: 1920
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation

                                                  Date: The incident occurred on 5 February 1922.


                                                  Place: Chauri Chaura, a small town in the Gorakhpur district of Uttar Pradesh (then United Provinces).

                                                  Background:

                                                  The event took place during the Non-Cooperation Movement launched by Mahatma Gandhi in 1920 against British rule.

                                                  The movement encouraged Indians to boycott British goods, schools, courts, and offices.

                                                  428

                                                  Total area of Asia continent

                                                  Correct Answer: 44.58 million km²
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation

                                                  The density of the world population as a whole is 38 persons per sq. km.

                                                  Asia has the highest density of 108 persons per square km

                                                  It comprises 60 percent of the population of the planet.

                                                  about 30% of Earth's total land area and 8% of Earth's total surface area

                                                  It is the most densely populated continent on the earth.

                                                  the total area of the Asia continent: is 44.58 million km²


                                                  while

                                                  Europe with 101 persons per square km ranks second.

                                                  ND4-5-2023

                                                  429

                                                  Who was the hero of the Pak-India 1965 war?

                                                  Correct Answer: Major Raja Aziz Bhatti
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation

                                                  Major Aziz Bhatti Shaheed directed his men to answer the fire until an enemy tank shell, which killed him on 12 September 1965, hit him.

                                                  He was 37 years old.
                                                  430

                                                  In which city is Shahbaz Air Base located in Pakistan?

                                                  Correct Answer: Jacobabad
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation

                                                  Shahbaz Air Base, also known as Jacobabad Air Base, is located in Jacobabad, a city in the Sindh province of Pakistan.

                                                  The base is operated by the Pakistan Air Force (PAF).

                                                  It serves both military and civilian aviation purposes.

                                                  431

                                                  Qasim port is located in which Province of Pakistan?

                                                  Correct Answer: Sindh
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation

                                                  Port Qasim is located in Karachi, Sindh,  Pakistan.

                                                  It is one of the busiest ports in Pakistan.

                                                  It is situated on the coast of the Arabian Sea.

                                                  It is approximately 35 kilometers east of Karachi's city center.

                                                  Opened: September 1980

                                                  432

                                                  Mamnoon Hussain was President of Pakistan from ______?

                                                  Correct Answer: 2013-2018
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation

                                                  Mamnoon Hussain served as the 12th President of Pakistan from 2013 to 2018.

                                                  He was first appointed Governor of Sindh in June 1999 by President Rafiq Tarar.

                                                  Mamnoon Hussain Died on 14 July 2021 (aged 80) in Karachi, Sindh.

                                                  ND23-4-2023

                                                  433

                                                  Quaid-e-Azam left the Indian National Congress in ______?

                                                  Correct Answer: 1920
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation

                                                  Jinnah joined the Indian National Congress in 1906.

                                                  Left congress 1920.

                                                  Join All India Muslim League in 1913.

                                                  Remain a member of both parties for 7 years.

                                                  434

                                                  Shafqat Amanat Ali’s band name is _______?

                                                  Correct Answer: Fuzon
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation

                                                  Shafqat Amanat Ali was the lead vocalist of the Pakistani band Fuzon.

                                                  He is known for blending classical and rock music. 

                                                  He gained fame with the band through songs like "Ankhon Ke Saagar" before pursuing a solo career.

                                                  435

                                                  Akbar born in UK (UmarKot) in _____?

                                                  Correct Answer: 1542
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation

                                                  Jalal uddin Muhammad Akbar was a Mughal emperor.

                                                  Akbar was born in Umarkot (Sindh) in 1542.

                                                  Akbar became emperor in 1556.

                                                  Akbar died in 1605.

                                                  436

                                                  Wazir Khan Mosque is located in _______?

                                                  Correct Answer: Lahore
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation

                                                  The Wazir Khan Mosque is a 17th-century mosque located in the city of Lahore.

                                                  The mosque was commissioned during the reign of the Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan.

                                                  Opened: 1642

                                                  ***

                                                  437

                                                  1999 War also called _____?

                                                  Correct Answer: Kargil War
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation

                                                  General Pervez Musharraf was the Chief of the Army Staff of the Pakistani army during the Kargil War in 1999.

                                                  while

                                                  Gen VP Malik was the Chief of the Army Staff of the Indian army during the Kargil war in 1999.

                                                  Related Question to the Kargil War:

                                                  The Kargil War was fought between India and Pakistan in 1999.

                                                  Kargil War fought from the month of May to July 1999

                                                  The Kargil War is also known as the Kargil Conflict but in India, this conflict is known as Operation Vijay

                                                  Kargil's Issue was stopped by the intervention of the United States of America (USA).

                                                  Strength: India: 30,000 = Pakistan: 5,000

                                                  The Kargil War was fought in the Kargil district of Kashmir and elsewhere along the Line of Control (LoC).

                                                  Captain Karnal Sher Khan Shaheed Nishan-e-Haider was known as the hero of the Kargil war

                                                  438

                                                  Mirza Ghalib was born in ______?

                                                  Correct Answer: Agra
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation
                                                  • Mirza Ghalib, one of the greatest Urdu and Persian poets.
                                                  • Birth Date: December 27, 1797.
                                                  • Birthplace: Kala Mahal, Agra, India.
                                                  • Original Name: Mirza Asadullah Beg Khan
                                                  • He passed away on 15 February 1869 in Delhi.
                                                  • Resting place: Mazar-e-Mirza Ghalib TombDelhi
                                                  • Pen name: Ghalib, Asad 
                                                  • Notable work: Diwan-e-Ghalib
                                                  • Mirza Abdullah Baig (father)
                                                  • Izzat-ut-Nisa Begum (mother)


                                                  Last updated by NS on 06 May 2026

                                                  439

                                                  Principles of Policy are covered under which Article of the Constitution of Pakistan?

                                                  Correct Answer: Article 29
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation

                                                  Article 29 outlines the Principles of Policy, guiding the state to promote welfare, justice, and equality, though not enforceable in court.


                                                  440

                                                  According to Article 24(2) of the Constitution, no person shall be deprived of property except?

                                                  Correct Answer: In accordance with law
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation

                                                  Article 24(2) of the Constitution of Pakistan ensures that property cannot be taken from a person unless done lawfully, i.e., as per legal procedures.

                                                  441

                                                  Nadir Shah invaded India during the reign of ______?

                                                  Correct Answer: Muhammad Shah
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation

                                                  Nadir Shah invaded India during the reign of Mughal emperor Muhammad Shah.

                                                  He ruled from 1719 to 1748.

                                                  On February 24, 1739, Nadir Shah's army defeated the Mughals at the Battle of Karnal.

                                                  Muhammad Shah surrendered, and Nadir Shah entered Delhi on March 20, 1739. 

                                                  442

                                                  India constructed the Kishanganga Dam on which river?

                                                  Correct Answer: Jhelum
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation

                                                  The Kishanganga dam diverts water from the Kishanganga River to a power plant in the Jhelum River basin

                                                  The Kishanganga Hydroelectric Project is a run-of-the-river hydroelectric scheme in Jammu and Kashmir, India.

                                                  The Kishanganga dam Opened: May 19, 2018

                                                  Installed capacity: 330 MW

                                                  The Kishanganga dam Height: 37 m

                                                  Owner: NHPC

                                                  NA21-8-2023

                                                  443

                                                  Who has become first Pakistani after 37 years to win World Junior Squash Championship?

                                                  Correct Answer: Hamza Khan
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation
                                                  • World Junior Squash Championship 2023
                                                  • Winner: Hamza Khan (Pakistan)
                                                  • Runner-Up: Mohamed Zakaria (Egypt)
                                                  • Venue: Melbourne, Australia
                                                  • Final held: 23 July 2023
                                                  • Note: This was Pakistan's first World Junior Squash title in nearly four decades as the last Pakistani player to lift this trophy was Jansher Khan, who won the title 37 years ago in 1986.
                                                  • *****
                                                  444

                                                  As per Pakistan army, which day is observed ‘Black Day’?

                                                  Correct Answer: May 9
                                                  Show explanation
                                                  Explanation

                                                  On 16 May 2023, National Security Committee decided to observe May 9 — when multiple incidents of violence and vandalism were reported amid protests sparked by PTI chief Imran Khan’s arrest — as “Black Day” at the national level.

                                                  445
